races.race_name: Boodles Anniversary 4-Y-O Juvenile Hurdle (Grade 1) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Maestro Conti
runners.spotlight: Untroubled to win sole start in France and scored in good style at Kempton (2m, good) and Cheltenham (Grade 2) in first two runs for Dan Skelton; well backed into 5-1 when runner-up in the Triumph Hurdle at Cheltenham (2m1f, soft) last month, when he travelled smoothly for a long way before keeping on well to edge out Minella Study in the battle for second; has leading claims on that form and he should be unfazed by this return to a sharper track.
runners.comment: Travelled smoothly for a long way when runner-up in the Triumph last month; key player

runners.name: Selma De Vary
runners.spotlight: Ran twice on Flat in France and three times in hurdle races at Auteuil, culminating in a win in November (2m1f, heavy); made a promising start for new yard when runner-up behind Narciso Has in a Grade 1 at Leopardstown in February; didn't come from so far back when fourth in the Triumph at Cheltenham last month but she made smooth progress and kept on well after being hampered in the closing stages; that makes her one of the form picks in this field and Willie Mullins has won this twice in the last three years.
runners.comment: Got hampered in closing stages when fourth in the Triumph; big player for Willie Mullins

runners.name: Mange Tout
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to multiple Grade 1 winner Impaire Et Passe; won her first three starts over hurdles before a third in Grade 1 at Leopardstown (2m, heavy) in February; may have more to offer in this sphere but she finished behind Selma De Vary last time and needs to resume progress.
runners.comment: Third in Grade 1 at Leopardstown but was behind Selma De Vary and needs to resume progress

runners.name: Minella Study
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden on the Flat but he's 3-4 over hurdles and travelled strongly for a long way when close third in the Triumph at Cheltenham last month; split Maestro Conti and Selma De Vary in that race and should make another bold bid here; that last run was on soft but his second win was on good.
runners.comment: Close third in the Triumph when he split Maestro Conti and Selma De Vary; respected

runners.name: Winston Junior
runners.spotlight: Useful maiden on the Flat at up 1m5f and he has a record of 2212 since switched to hurdling for his current yard; seems to be learning to settle better and he didn't get a smooth run when an eye-catching second in the juvenile handicap at Cheltenham (2m, good to soft) last month; that was another good step forward and he could continue to progress in this sphere; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Second in the juvenile handicap at Cheltenham and he's open to more progress; interesting
runners.owner: R Bartlett, J Carthy & Mrs P Shanahan

runners.name: Ammes
runners.spotlight: Good Flat horse who won his first two starts over hurdles before a close second behind Minella Study in a Listed race at Wetherby (2m, good) in October; still has potential in this sphere but he was only 12th in the juvenile handicap at Cheltenham last month and needs to resume his progress in this tough race; visor added.
runners.comment: Listed runner-up but he was well held in the juvenile handicap at Cheltenham last month

runners.name: Aqua Bleu
runners.spotlight: Promising second on debut at Hereford (2m, good) in November and he's unbeaten in three runs since; has looked a very willing front-runner but all his wins have been in small-field events and he has a lot to find in this Grade 1 contest.
runners.comment: Front-runner who is 3-4 over hurdles but he has a lot to find in this Grade 1 contest

runners.name: Indian River
runners.spotlight: Promise in his two Irish Flat maidens; justified favouritism in first three starts over hurdles but his limitations were exposed when a never-dangerous tenth in the Triumph at Cheltenham (50-1) last month; passed over.
runners.comment: Triple hurdle winner but his limitations were exposed in the Triumph (tenth) last month

runners.name: Lord
runners.spotlight: Made it 4-6 over hurdles when returning to form with an all-the-way win in a Listed event over C&D (soft) in December; ran to a similar level when runner-up in the Scottish Triumph at Musselburgh in January but this is a much tougher test and he has plenty to find on the figures; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: 4-7 over hurdles but this is a tough test and he has plenty to find on the figures

runners.name: Wolf Rayet
runners.spotlight: Three Flat runs in Ireland and four over hurdles in Britain; ran well at a massive price in the Triumph at Cheltenham (eighth of 20) last month but he was beaten 10l and finished behind three of today's rivals; others preferred.
runners.comment: Ran well at a massive price in the Triumph but beaten 10l and he was behind three of these

races.race_name: William Hill Manifesto Novices' Chase (Grade 1) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lulamba
runners.spotlight: Acts on any ground; ran creditably on the figures but was only third when favourite for the Arkle, in which a mistake two out cost him some ground when challenging; has otherwise been a rising force at about 2m and his record away from the Cheltenham Festival is 6-6, comfortably beating his elders in the Game Spirit for the most recent success; gives the firm impression he's ready for a crack at this new trip; sets the standard and retains the potential for rating higher still.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite in the Arkle; has otherwise been a rising star; this new trip should suit

runners.name: Blueking d'Oroux
runners.spotlight: Useful 2m4f novice chaser but he won a substandard edition of the Rising Stars at Wincanton and his close second at Kempton (with cheekpieces fitted) last time was achieved in a handicap; faces a stiffer task at this level in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Won a substandard Rising Stars; close second in handicap last time; this is a stiffer task

runners.name: Koktail Divin
runners.spotlight: Non-stayer (shaped better than the bare result) in the 3m1f Brown Advisory at Cheltenham having surprisingly swerved the 2m4f Jack Richards option; broadly progressive otherwise and scored by a wide margin at Leopardstown (2m5f, yielding) the time before, recording a very useful RPR; ran well in the Mersey over hurdles at this meeting 12 months ago; remains of major interest, back down in distance.
runners.comment: Non-stayer in the 3m1f Brown Advisory; broadly progressive otherwise; remains of interest

runners.name: Jax Junior
runners.spotlight: Finished 18l behind Lulamba in the Arkle but may fare better in this rematch, with the return to further a likely plus; readily bagged Grade 2 honours at Kempton (2m4f, good to soft) the time before, enhancing his good strike-rate when racing beyond 2m.
runners.comment: Has a good strike-rate when racing beyond 2m, the most recent success in Kempton Grade 2

runners.name: Mambonumberfive
runners.spotlight: Comes here fresh having bypassed the Cheltenham Festival and been absent since a Kingmaker defeat that is perhaps best forgiven due to the heavy ground; 3-3 over fences otherwise, including 2m wins at Aintree (handicap) and Kempton (Grade 2, readily); may resume improvement, provided he stays the new trip.
runners.comment: Best to forgive latest effort; 3-3 over fences otherwise; may resume improvement; new trip

races.race_name: Racing Welfare Bowl Chase (Grade 1) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jango Baie
runners.spotlight: Kept on for close third to Impaire Et Passe in the 2m4f Manifesto here 12 months ago and has improved for longer trips this season; very close fourth in thrilling finish to the King George at Kempton (3m, good) in December and 8l second in the Gold Cup at Cheltenham (3m2f, good to soft) last month; leading claims if that hard race 27 days ago hasn't left a mark.
runners.comment: Runner-up in the Gold Cup; leading claims if that hard race 27 days ago hasn't left a mark

runners.name: Protektorat
runners.spotlight: 11yo whose last Grade 1 win came in March 2024 but he showed strong form when keeping on for second of four to Jonbon in the Melling Chase here (2m4f, good) 12 months ago and has won at Windsor (2m6f, soft; warm conditions race) and Kelso (extended 2m7f, good to soft; Listed) on his two runs this year; he should still be fine at this sort of trip and could be a contender.
runners.comment: 11yo; no Grade 1 win since March 2024 but still capable of strong form; possible contender
runners.owner: Sir A Ferguson, G Mason, L Hales, The Late J Hales

runners.name: Impaire Et Passe
runners.spotlight: Unproven at this trip and reportedly never travelling when pulled up in the Ryanair at Cheltenham last month; however, he's won at this meeting in each of the last two seasons, landing the Grade 1 Manifesto Novices' Chase last April (2m4f, good to soft; Jango Baie third), and he produced a top performance in the Grade 2 Red Mills Chase at Gowran Park this February; could bounce back with big run for last year's winning trainer Willie Mullins.
runners.comment: Pulled up in the Ryanair but 2-2 at this meeting and could bounce back with a big run

runners.name: Pic d'Orhy
runners.spotlight: 11yo who looked as good as ever when runner-up in a captivating battle with Jonbon in the Ascot Chase (2m5f, good to soft) in February, when bidding to win that Grade 1 for the third consecutive year; won the Grade 1 Melling Chase here (2m4f, soft) in 2023; seemed fine at 3m when 1l second in this season's Charlie Hall (good) and his trainer has won this race six times; no surprise to see a bold bid from this front-runner, who is not ruled out.
runners.comment: 11yo; three Grade 1 wins (including here); looked as good as ever last time; not ruled out

runners.name: Spillane's Tower
runners.spotlight: Won two Grade 1s in Ireland in spring 2024 during novice chase campaign (2m4f/3m1f, heavy/yielding) and posted first win since in the Grade 2 Cotswold Chase at Cheltenham (3m1f, soft) this January; he was receiving 6lb from close runner-up L'Homme Presse last time and others in this have achieved more, but he's only eight and could still have improvement to come.
runners.comment: Won Grade 2 Cotswold Chase; others have achieved more but he's open to further improvement

races.race_name: Randox Foxhunters' Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Barton Snow
runners.spotlight: Proved his stamina and made it 5-5 under rules (all in hunter chases) when beating Its On The Line by a neck to win the big one at Cheltenham (3m2f, good to soft) last month, despite a mistake at the final fence; has also won on good going; he doesn't appear to be straightforward but clearly knows how to win and should be fine back down in trip; major player.
runners.comment: Won the big one at Cheltenham last month; should be fine back down in trip; major player

runners.name: Its On The Line
runners.spotlight: Won this in 2024 (heavy); beaten a neck by Barton Snow at last month's Cheltenham Festival (3m2f, good to soft), finishing a close second in that hunter chase for the fourth season in a row; at this trip he may prefer more testing ground than is likely today, but he's still entitled to respect.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024; went close again at Cheltenham last month and he's entitled to respect

runners.name: Unexpected Party
runners.spotlight: Won the Grand Annual at Cheltenham Festival in March 2024 (2m, soft) and runner-up in it last year (good to soft); comfortable wins in hunter chases on last two starts (2m5f/2m6f, good/good to soft); his stamina will be more severely tested in today's hotter company but leading claims if seeing it out.
runners.comment: Comfortable wins over 2m5f/2m6f on last two starts; holds leading form claims

runners.name: Golden Son
runners.spotlight: Useful handicapper who posted easy wins this year in his first two hunter chases (3m/2m7f, good to soft/heavy); 14l fourth at the Cheltenham Festival last month (3m2f, good to soft) so has work to do with some of these, but he was left behind up the hill and is not ruled out back down in trip.
runners.comment: 14l fourth at Cheltenham last month; work to do but the drop back in trip may help
runners.owner: G Mason, A Ferguson And P Nicholls

runners.name: Gracchus De Balme
runners.spotlight: Pulled up at the Cheltenham Festival (3m2f, good to soft) last month; however, he was also pulled up at Leicester last February prior to winning this race at 22-1 (good to soft) so he's capable of bouncing back.
runners.comment: Pulled up at Cheltenham Festival but won this last year and he's capable of bouncing back

runners.name: Snipe
runners.spotlight: Won on his first hunter chase start at Hereford (2m5f, soft) in January and bounced back from a heavy defeat behind Golden Son at Taunton (2m7f, heavy) to win at Leicester (2m, good to soft) last month; however, he needs a clear career best if he's to win this.
runners.comment: 2-3 in hunter chases but he needs a clear career best if he's to win this

runners.name: Thunder Rock
runners.spotlight: Very talented in his prime; this 10yo isn't the force of old and today's race is much tougher than the hunter chases he's won easily on his last two starts (2m4f, good to soft/good) but if he can tap further into his back-class he may well be in the mix; James King has won this twice.
runners.comment: Very talented in his prime; easy hunter chase wins on last two starts; could be in the mix

runners.name: Music Drive
runners.spotlight: He's made the frame at 33-1 in the last two runnings of the Cheltenham Festival hunter chase, finishing about 8l behind Barton Snow and Its On The Line last month when third; tackles a new track today and the drop back in trip isn't an obvious positive, but he could still be an each-way contender.
runners.comment: Again made the frame at 33-1 in the Cheltenham Festival hunter chase; each-way contender

runners.name: Hollywood Harmon
runners.spotlight: Easily won a maiden hunter chase at Cartmel (2m5f, good to soft) last May; didn't run badly at Fakenham (3m, soft) in February but well beaten at Carlisle (2m5f, good to soft) since and she would be a shock winner.
runners.comment: Won maiden hunter chase at Cartmel last May but she would be a shock winner today

runners.name: Willewonga
runners.spotlight: Pulled up at 50-1 in this last year and same outcome at the Cheltenham Festival last month, when 100-1; however, that latest outing was his first run for 110 days and he may have needed it; in front when making a bad blunder at the final fence at Bangor (3m, soft) last February and that form suggests he might not be a forlorn hope each-way, at what should be a big price.
runners.comment: Pulled up at Cheltenham last month but after a break; might not be a forlorn hope each-way

runners.name: Lets Go Champ
runners.spotlight: Won a competitive handicap chase at the Punchestown festival in May 2024 for Henry de Bromhead (2m4f, good to yielding) and winning a hunter chase at Gowran Park (3m1f, heavy) last month showed that he retains considerable ability at the age of 11; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Beat a very solid yardstick for last month's hunter chase win and firmly in calculations

runners.name: Java Point
runners.spotlight: Useful, four-time handicap chase winner who scored at Ludlow (3m, good to soft) in January on his first hunter chase start; runner-up on next two outings (3m/2m7f, soft/good to soft); this 11yo needs a bigger run today.
runners.comment: 11yo who has been in good form since tackling hunter chases but needs a bigger run today

runners.name: A Moments Madness
runners.spotlight: Progressive in handicap chases last spring for Warren Greatrex (2m2f/2m3f, good/soft) and last month's third on stable debut in a hunter chase at Carlisle (2m5f, good to soft), after an absence, can be upgraded as he hung left at that right-handed track; has plenty to find but this 7yo is very lightly raced and, now going left-handed, could go well at sizeable odds.
runners.comment: His comeback third at Carlisle can be upgraded; could go well at sizeable odds

runners.name: Great Notions
runners.spotlight: Won maiden hunter chase at Hexham (3m, good) last May; some good runs in defeat in handicaps since November, most recently 5l second at Sedgefield (3m2f, good), but he has plenty to find today.
runners.comment: Some good runs in defeat in handicaps since November but plenty to find today

runners.name: Densworth
runners.spotlight: Showed the odd snippet of very useful form in 2024, winning easily at Wetherby (2m3f, good) that November on his second chase start; however, his subsequent exploits put him well down the pecking order in this line-up.
runners.comment: The odd snippet of useful form in 2024 but down the pecking order on subsequent evidence

runners.name: Take All
runners.spotlight: Good efforts in defeat behind Java Point (3m, good to soft) and Unexposed Party (2m6f, good to soft) this year and ran well for a long way before fading at last month's Cheltenham Festival (3m2f); could have an each-way shout at big odds now back down in trip; reapplied cheekpieces replace hood.
runners.comment: Ran well for a long way at Cheltenham; could have an each-way shout now back down in trip

runners.name: Fairly Famous
runners.spotlight: Strong form when winning hunter chases at Cheltenham in May 2023 and May 2024; in good heart in point-to-points this season but last May's hunter chase form suggests he may be found wanting.
runners.comment: In good heart in points but last May's hunter chase form suggests he's up against it

runners.name: A Jet Of Our Own
runners.spotlight: Very hard to fancy judged on this year's hunter chase form but he took a big step forward when fifth of 29 at 125-1 in this race last April (good to soft) and is not ruled out each-way back over these obstacles.
runners.comment: Hard to fancy on this year's form but ran well when fifth of 29 at 125-1 in this last term

runners.name: Fan De Blues
runners.spotlight: Very useful for Willie Mullins and ran pretty well over these fences in the 2023 Topham; following a long absence this 11yo hasn't been devoid of promise for his new yard, but needs to take a significant step forward.
runners.comment: Very useful in his prime; not devoid of promise for this yard but needs a step forward

runners.name: Famous Clermont
runners.spotlight: Won this in 2023 (good to soft) and bagged further hunter chase wins in the same Stratford race in May 2024 and 2025 (2m6f, good); won a point last month and he's better than he showed when tailed off in this last April, but this 11yo probably isn't as good as he once was and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Won this in 2023 but this 11yo probably isn't as good as he once was; others are preferred

runners.name: Joker De Mai
runners.spotlight: 7-1 when early faller at the Chair in this last year; this 7yo was an eye-catching third at Ludlow in February on penultimate start, having been hampered, but needs to step up on this season's handicap form if he's to threaten.
runners.comment: Caught the eye two starts ago but he needs to step up on this season's handicap form

runners.name: Gaboriot
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in the Becher here in December and his two subsequent hunter chase runs have yielded an early fall and disappointing defeat; twice placed over these fences, however, most recently in the Grand Sefton over C&D in November, and could have an each-way shout if back on song in change of headgear.
runners.comment: Return to form needed but twice placed over these fences and gets a change of headgear

runners.name: Empire De Maulde
runners.spotlight: Four point wins last year and runner-up in two hunter chases last spring (2m7f/2m6f, good to soft/good); however, he'll need to improve upon that form and this 12yo hasn't shone on either outing this year.
runners.comment: Four point wins last year but this 12yo hasn't shone in 2026

runners.name: Whats The Solution
runners.spotlight: 6-21 in points, his latest win in that sphere coming last month; 12l third of six to Barton Snow at Stratford (2m6f, good) last May in his sole hunter chase and he looks up against it.
runners.comment: Ran okay last May in his sole hunter chase but looks up against it today

runners.name: Enqarde
runners.spotlight: Career highlight was the Tommy Whittle at Haydock in 2021; 50-1 winner of four-runner hunter chase at Leicester (2m6f, soft) this February but pulled up at Exeter (3m, good to soft) since; this 12yo is probably vulnerable.
runners.comment: 50-1 winner at Leicester in February but pulled up at Exeter since; probably vulnerable

races.race_name: William Hill Aintree Hurdle (Grade 1) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Brighterdaysahead
runners.spotlight: Has form on most types of ground; took her Cheltenham Festival record to 0-3 with a defeat in the latest Champion Hurdle but that was a respectable effort without managing to confirm placings with Lossiemouth, who had played second fiddle in the Irish equivalent; record of 2-3 at about 2m4f includes an easy success in the Mersey at this meeting two years ago; very smart mare who holds leading claims at the weights.
runners.comment: Very smart mare; easy winner at this meeting in 2024; has leading claims at the weights

runners.name: The New Lion
runners.spotlight: Broadly progressive; has gone down the Champion Hurdle route this season and ran well in that race most recently, while leaving the impression he's ready for a return to further than 2m; leading novice hurdler at about 2m4f last term, winning the Challow and the Turners (both on good to soft ground); still unbeaten over this sort of distance and commands respect.
runners.comment: Ran well in the Champion Hurdle; leading novice at about 2m4f last term; commands respect

runners.name: Alexei
runners.spotlight: Acts on any ground; good strike-rate includes wins in the Greatwood and Kingwell this season; ties in with Brighterdaysahead and The New Lion on Champion Hurdle running where a late blunder cost him ground when challenging for the places; has form at up to 2m3f; remains a progressive sort.
runners.comment: Good strike-rate; ran well in the Champion Hurdle and remains a progressive sort

runners.name: Lucky Place
runners.spotlight: Went for a handicap option at the latest Cheltenham Festival and was well beaten, albeit with the race not panning out totally smoothly; dual Grade 2 winner last term but still needs to prove he's a Grade 1 hurdler and has the worst chance at the weights in this field.
runners.comment: Still needs to prove he's a Grade 1 hurdler and has the worst chance at the weights
runners.owner: Mrs G Van Geest & M George

runners.name: Golden Ace
runners.spotlight: Fortuitous winner of the 2025 Champion Hurdle (same story in the Fighting Fifth) and finished only fifth, behind some of today's rivals, when attempting to retain her crown; largely consistent and has won over 2m4f but increasing her Grade 1 tally may require further luck.
runners.comment: Registered her Grade 1 wins in fortuitous fashion and is going to need further luck

runners.name: El Fabiolo
runners.spotlight: Probably acts on any going; high class over fences until jumping issues developed; has proved suited by the return to hurdles and an easy win at Leopardstown (2m2f) last time took his record in this sphere to 4-7; the best horse in this field if viewed on his peak chase form.
runners.comment: Latest win took hurdles record to 4-7; the best horse in this if viewed on peak chase form

runners.name: Potters Charm
runners.spotlight: Acts on any ground; the freshest contender in this race, having bypassed the Cheltenham Festival and been absent since landing the National Spirit at Fontwell (2m3f) over six weeks ago when taking hurdles record to 7-11; sole attempt at Aintree resulted in a Grade 1 novice success on Boxing Day 2024; likely to go well.
runners.comment: Record of 7-11 over hurdles; 1-1 at Aintree (Gr 1 novice) and he's the freshest contender

races.race_name: Close Brothers Red Rum Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Sans Bruit
runners.spotlight: His exploits earlier in the season aren't of much concern given his last five wins have come in the month of April, including the last two runnings of this race (soft/good to soft; has also won on good); this prominent racer did it comfortably 12 months ago and is just 3lb higher today; could once more take all the beating.
runners.comment: Has won last two runnings of this race; did it comfortably last year when just 3lb lower

runners.name: Javert Allen
runners.spotlight: Below par at Kempton (2m4f) in February last time but he's run well in defeat otherwise this season, including when fourth over C&D (good to soft) on his reappearance in a race that worked out extremely well; this front-runner could make a bold bid in first-time cheekpieces back at 2m.
runners.comment: Below par last time but in good form otherwise this season and could make a bold bid

runners.name: Highlands Legacy
runners.spotlight: Won on chase debut at Worcester (2m, good) in October and runner-up on next two starts (including C&D) before narrow win at Windsor (2m, soft) in January; he's 6lb higher than last time and in a hotter race today, but this progressive 7yo may be able to find the necessary further improvement.
runners.comment: Enjoying a progressive first campaign over fences; every chance he'll continue to improve

runners.name: Petit Tonnerre
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten ninth in this 12 months ago (good to soft) and tailed off on his last three starts; however, the return to better ground can help and he won a big-field handicap at Punchestown (2m, yielding) last May; on a handy mark judged on that Irish form and he's not discounted.
runners.comment: Tailed off on his last three starts but not discounted in view of last May's Irish win

runners.name: Hercule Du Seuil
runners.spotlight: Won Grade 3 at Killarney (2m4f, yielding) last May for Willie Mullins and he's on a handy mark on that form; however, below par in the winter on final two starts for that yard and soundly beaten in handicaps on both outings for this stable; usual hood is left off; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Ex-Willie Mullins; won Grade 3 chase last May but hard to fancy on more recent evidence

runners.name: Jasko Des Dames
runners.spotlight: Returned to form back on better ground when fifth, beaten just over 10l, in the Grand Annual at Cheltenham (2m, good); he led jumping two out at the Festival and this less demanding track could be right up his street.
runners.comment: Led jumping 2 out in the Grand Annual (when 5th); this less demanding track could be ideal

runners.name: Grain d'Oudairies
runners.spotlight: 10yo who hasn't had much racing for his age and has been progressive this term, with wins at Kelso (2m1f, good to soft) and Haydock (2m4f, good to soft); however, below par at Carlisle in February last time and, even if back on song, there's a firm suspicion this will prove too competitive.
runners.comment: 10yo who has enjoyed a progressive season but this may prove too competitive

runners.name: Inthepocket
runners.spotlight: His fortunes over fences have been mixed but he made good headway in the Grand Annual at Cheltenham (2m, good) last month prior to falling two out; finest hour came when winning Grade 1 Top Novices' Hurdle here (2m, good to soft) in April 2023; could have a big say if avoiding mishap.
runners.comment: Made headway in the Grand Annual prior to falling two out; his finest hour came here

runners.name: Dr T J Eckleburg
runners.spotlight: Runs off a mark 10lb lower than when fifth of 14 in this last year, beaten just over 13l; he's run a couple of reasonable races this season but hasn't shown enough to suggest he's the one to be on today.
runners.comment: 10lb lower than when fifth in this last year but others arrive with more pressing claims

runners.name: Wonleg
runners.spotlight: Won Wexford handicap (2m, good) last July on third chase start and returned to form when third of 20 at Leopardstown (2m1f, yielding) in December; needs a career best today but this unexposed 6yo represents a top Irish trainer (who saddles three) and could yet have more to offer.
runners.comment: Unexposed 6yo who represents a top Irish trainer and may yet have more to offer

runners.name: Palamon
runners.spotlight: Well-backed, even-money favourite at Bangor (2m4f) last Tuesday, when reverting to good ground in a reapplied tongue-tie and returning to form with a convincing win; 9lb higher in a much hotter race today but not ruled out for his top yard on the back of that stylish success.
runners.comment: Well backed when returning to form with ready win at Bangor; not ruled out off 9lb higher

runners.name: Brookie
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in Ascot handicap in November and tailed off on sole outing since in the Champion Chase last month; it's hard to say what sort of form he's in but this C&D brought out the very best in him when runner-up in the Grade 1 Maghull Novices' Chase (good) 12 months ago; could go well back here.
runners.comment: Hard to say what sort of form he's in but this C&D brought out very best in him last April

runners.name: Ryan's Rocket
runners.spotlight: Firmly in the picture judged on his handicap win at Newbury (2m, good to soft) in November and he drops back in trip today having weakened over 2m4f at Kempton last month; not ruled out but he made mistakes last time and has unseated on two of his last four starts; needs to cut out the blunders.
runners.comment: Firmly in the picture on his November win but his jumping might be put under pressure here

runners.name: Stencil
runners.spotlight: 5yo; trained in France but campaigned in Britain since start of 2025; impressed in a warm novice chase at Chepstow (2m, soft) in January and 25l tenth in the Jack Richards at Cheltenham (2m4f, good) was better than the bare facts, as he pulled hard and was held up in a race that favoured those who were up with the pace; could settle better now back down in trip; interesting.
runners.comment: 5yo who ran better than the bare facts at Cheltenham; interesting now back down in trip

runners.name: Boothill
runners.spotlight: Ascot specialist who runs at Aintree for the first time; following wind surgery he ran okay when 13l eighth of 20 in the Grand Annual at Cheltenham (2m, good) last month, and he's another 4lb lower today; however, this 11yo is without a win since 2023 and needs to find something extra now in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 8th in the Grand Annual and 4lb lower today, but this 11yo needs to find something extra

races.race_name: Goffs Nickel Coin Mares' Standard Open National Hunt Flat Race (Grade 2) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Ti'mamzel
runners.spotlight: Above-average mare whose strong record (all on soft ground) comprises a good second against males at Windsor flanked by two Listed wins, latest at Sandown where she did it in emphatic fashion; brings major claims to this next step on the ladder.
runners.comment: Strong record features two Listed wins, latest in emphatic style at Sandown; major player

runners.name: Kiltybo
runners.spotlight: Swallowed up in the Thurles race won by Lennon Grove on rules debut but has made all in both starts since and the figure she recorded at Naas (soft to heavy) last time gives her possibilities upped in class; an easy lead isn't as certain back in a bigger field, however.
runners.comment: Has possibilities on the figures but another easy lead is not guaranteed

runners.name: Nan's Choice
runners.spotlight: Has a useful pedigree; won by 5l at Gowran Park (heavy) in the latest of her two outings for Conor Houlihan; sold for £115,000 the following week and has joined Dan Skelton; interesting contender, with any amount of further improvement a possibility.
runners.comment: Sold for £115,000 since winning by 5l at Gowran Park; has joined Dan Skelton; interesting

runners.name: Princess Day
runners.spotlight: Remains unbeaten, having won her sole attempt in the Irish point-to-point scene and both starts under rules; beat Fairy Park in great style at Newbury (good to soft) most recently, looking a mare who has plausible Graded aspirations; commands respect.
runners.comment: Won well at Newbury last time; remains unbeaten and has plausible Graded aspirations

runners.name: Seven Stars
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism at Hereford (good) last time, beating Roxy Russet by 3l and building on a couple of solid efforts; bred to do better still, being related to several winners for her stable, including two mares who were placed in this contest; strong credentials.
runners.comment: Likely to build on her Hereford win; related to two mares who were placed in this race

runners.name: Lennon Grove
runners.spotlight: Opened her rules account in Thurles event and has since performed well in the Sandown Listed contest (soft) won by Ti'mamzel; this Irish mare brings progressive RPRs, which suggests another good run is likely.
runners.comment: Ran well in the Sandown race won by Ti'mamzel; this Irish mare has progressive RPRs

runners.name: Tiktok Casey
runners.spotlight: Irish mare who has belied 20-1 and 28-1 odds since switched to rules, rallying to win at Naas (soft to heavy) then good third in Grade 2 Leopardstown event at the Dublin Racing Festival (heavy); seems likely to give supporters a good run for their money.
runners.comment: Rallied to win at Naas on rules debut and ran well in Grade 2 at Leopardstown

runners.name: Burds Of A Feather
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-2 in completed starts, namely wide-margin wins on final attempt in Irish points and on rules debut; went freely to post and in the race at Warwick (soft) but wasn't for catching, having soon established a clear lead; withdrawn when 4-1 for Sandown Listed event since, having bolted before the start; has potential provided her fizzy ways can be tamed.
runners.comment: Fizzy sort but has potential; wasn't for catching at Warwick on rules debut

runners.name: Fairy Park
runners.spotlight: Easily justified favouritism in an Irish point and fared best of the rest behind Princess Day at Newbury (good to soft); could go well, albeit unlikely to reverse placings with that rival who remains unbeaten.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; fared best of the rest behind unbeaten Princess Day at Newbury

runners.name: Emerald Quest
runners.spotlight: Avoided trouble and scored easily in an incident-packed race at Huntingdon (soft) with tongue-tie applied on return from lengthy absence; had some luck but breezed clear in a manner that suggests she is potentially useful; one to consider.
runners.comment: Had some luck at Huntingdon but breezed clear in the manner of a potentially useful mare

runners.name: Peace Belle
runners.spotlight: Far from disgraced at Newcastle when carrying a penalty for her Hexham success; however, looks well short of Graded standard; yard also runs It Would Be You.
runners.comment: Won at Hexham then not disgraced at Newcastle; this is much harder

runners.name: Martini Majesty
runners.spotlight: Related to a very notable Flat stayer for her yard; it's hard to get excited about the form of her Ascot win (good to soft; beat One Dimensional by 2l) but she should be capable of progress and helps to give Alan King an interesting hand.
runners.comment: Ascot scorer who should progress and helps to give Alan King an interesting hand

runners.name: One Dimensional
runners.spotlight: Has consistent RPRs but faces a much stiffer assignment upped in grade and gives the impression, backed up by pedigree, that she's crying out for jumps races over further.
runners.comment: Faces a stiff task and is crying out for jumps races over further

runners.name: It Would Be You
runners.spotlight: Well on top at the finish in Catterick event (good) and looks an interesting prospect for Jennie Candlish; goes into much deeper waters but could make waves with significant progress possible after one run.
runners.comment: Well on top at Catterick and looks an interesting prospect for Jennie Candlish

runners.name: Ladies Day
runners.spotlight: Point bumper winner whose consistent rules form has culminated in second-place finishes at Aintree (failed only narrowly) and Huntingdon (Listed; behind Ti'mamzel); again likely to give her running.
runners.comment: Point bumper winner who has consistent rules form; second last time to Ti'mamzel

runners.name: Divine Diva
runners.spotlight: Got the better of Lennon Grove in an Irish point and bagged a bronze medal in Listed grade at Cheltenham; however, needs to show progress and Harry Skelton duly prefers interesting new stablemate Nan's Choice.
runners.comment: Needs to show progress; Harry Skelton duly prefers new stablemate Nan's Choice

runners.name: Roxy Russet
runners.spotlight: Ran well behind Seven Stars at Hereford, showing that her fall in the Huntingdon race won by Emerald Quest had left no scars but taking record to 0-3; seems out of her depth at this level.
runners.comment: Ran well behind Seven Stars last time but looks out of her depth at this level

runners.name: Midnight Musical
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has posted a couple of duck eggs since finishing second at the Galway festival on rules debut; first-time headgear needs to have a major effect.
runners.comment: Has gone the wrong way since finishing second at Galway; headgear is now applied

runners.name: Billey Shiftit
runners.spotlight: Brings the worst form having finished only sixth of 14 in Wincanton contest; may do better in due course but this looks an impossible task.
runners.comment: Brings the worst form having finished only sixth at Wincanton

runners.name: Brave Lady
runners.spotlight: Absent since showing promise with third of 15, one place behind Midnight Musical, at last year's Galway festival (on good ground); open to improvement but this presents a demanding return to action.
runners.comment: Absent since showing promise with third of 15 at last year's Galway festival

races.race_name: Frodon & Black Corton At Taunton Novices' Hurdle (Novices' Championship Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Minella Yoga
runners.spotlight: Bought for £360,000 after scoring over hurdles at Fairyhouse in November; has created a very good impression for his new yard, following up in Newbury novice (2m) before finishing third of ten in Grade 2 at Cheltenham in January; the one to beat back after a break here.
runners.comment: Excellent third in Grade 2 at Cheltenham in January; the one to beat back after a break

runners.name: Secret Sniper
runners.spotlight: A fair bumper performer who shaped well when a clear second of 12 in 2m Huntingdon novice hurdle 22 days ago; this brother to the stable's useful winner Secret Squirrel has more to offer and shouldn't be underestimated.
runners.comment: Shaped well when second at Huntingdon latest; more to offer; shouldn't be underestimated

runners.name: Lau And Shaz
runners.spotlight: Failed to score for Gavin Cromwell but she made it 2-3 for her current yard in 2m Hereford novice hurdle 57 days ago; this is tougher but she scored cosily there.
runners.comment: Cosily made it 2-3 for current yard at Hereford 57 days ago; this is tougher however

runners.name: Itsinthename
runners.spotlight: Order Of St George gelding; placed in an Irish point and Lingfield bumper (for Nigel and Willy Twiston-Davies) before landing 2m Catterick maiden hurdler for new yard in February; built on that when sixth in 2m4f Newbury novice 20 days ago but this demands another step forward.
runners.comment: Won at Catterick and built on that with Newbury sixth 20 days ago; this demands more

runners.name: Corsican Caper
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m6f Flat winner; built on his hurdling debut seventh when third of four in 2m Doncaster novice 27 days ago but this demands considerably more.
runners.comment: A fair 1m6f Flat winner; third at Doncaster latest but this is a much tougher ask

runners.name: Regal Icon
runners.spotlight: 100-1, pulled up on his hurdling debut at Wincanton (1m7f, soft) in January; it's easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Pulled up on hurdling debut at Wincanton in January; significantly more is required

runners.name: Hawa Jumeirah
runners.spotlight: A dual Flat scorer for Ismail Mohammed in the first half of 2025 but he offered little in three subsequent runs in that sphere for Robbie Llewellyn and again on his yard/hurdling debut at Wincanton (1m7f) in February.
runners.comment: Offered little on his yard/hurdling debut at Wincanton (1m7f) in February

races.race_name: C&D South West Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Happy Returns
runners.spotlight: Gained her breakthrough success at Wincanton (3m) in December but she's been well below par both runs since, pulled up at Huntingdon (25f) 22 days ago.
runners.comment: Gained breakthrough win at Wincanton in December but well below par both runs since

runners.name: Noble Blue
runners.spotlight: Remains winless under rules but he was a multiple points winner after leaving Paul Nolan in Ireland and has started well for his new yard, a strong-travelling second of 11 in 2m6f handicap hurdle at Exeter 23 days ago; can go one better here.
runners.comment: Multiple points winner; very good second at Exeter 23 days ago; can go one better here

runners.name: Marhaba Million
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m4f Flat maiden for Owen Burrows; yet to show that level of form in this sphere but took a step forward when sixth of 12 in 2m4f Wincanton handicap 17 days ago; possibilities off a slipping mark.
runners.comment: Took a step forward when sixth at Wincanton 17 days ago; possibilities off a slipping mark

runners.name: Gasmani
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 7yo; looked rusty after eight months off when last of nine in 3m Ascot handicap hurdle in December; needs to take a major step forward if he's to gain a first victory in this sphere.
runners.comment: Off eight months before last at Ascot in December; needs to take a major step forward

runners.name: Saint Polo
runners.spotlight: Brought along steadily before taking a big step forward on his handicap debut when a clear second of seven at Sedgefield (2m4f) last month; up 5lb but remains low mileage and has his first go over this trip.
runners.comment: Clear second at Sedgefield latest; up 5lb but first go over this trip here so in the mix

races.race_name: Tony & Carole Stocker Anniversary Surprise Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Bluenose Belle
runners.spotlight: Bagged a pair of handicaps (around 2m) at Fontwell and Market Rasen last spring; lightly raced since but not discredited given she was set plenty to do when fifth of nine at Huntingdon (2m) 29 days ago; considered off a 2lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Not discredited when fifth at Huntingdon 29 days ago; considered off a 2lb lower mark

runners.name: Just A Heartbeat
runners.spotlight: Fontwell runner-up on her hurdling debut last May but she's twice failed to build on it, hooded when ninth in 2m Newbury novice in November; needs this switch to handicaps to spark improvement after a break.
runners.comment: Fontwell runner-up on her hurdling debut last May but she's twice failed to build on it

runners.name: Miracles Do Happen
runners.spotlight: A fair maiden hurdler but she went too freely on her first go in handicaps when only sixth of eight at Hereford (2m) last month; tongue tie is added.
runners.comment: Too free when only sixth of eight at Hereford (2m) last month; tongue tie is added

runners.name: Ask Peter
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m6f Flat winner but she's yet to match that form in this sphere and came in a below-par seventh in 2m3f handicap here 56 days ago; more is required.
runners.comment: A fair 1m6f Flat winner but she's yet to match that form in this sphere; more is required

runners.name: Little Venice
runners.spotlight: Quirky sort but she got off the mark in this sphere in 1m7f handicap at Wincanton 43 days ago, despite being reluctant at the start; up 4lb but she still merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Wincanton latest; quirky and up 4lb but still merits serious consideration

runners.name: Dance Havana
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m6f Flat winner in 2023 for Alice Haynes; lightly raced over hurdles but she wasn't discredited after a two-year absence when third of 13 over C&D 16 days ago; possibilities.
runners.comment: Not discredited after two-year absence when third over C&D 16 days ago; possibilities

runners.name: Aquifolia
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden and she went in snatches when a fair sixth of 14 in 2m Worcester handicap in October; returns from a break in a refitted hood.
runners.comment: Fair sixth in 2m Worcester handicap in October; returns from a break in a refitted hood

runners.name: Night Burlesque
runners.spotlight: Fair form shown in bumpers and when third on her hurdling debut at Warwick (2m) in October; has failed to go on though and she was tried in a hood when beating only one on her handicap debut at Wetherby (2m) last time.
runners.comment: Third on hurdling debut at Warwick but has failed to go on, hooded at Wetherby latest run

runners.name: For Love Of Beauty
runners.spotlight: Only poor form shown thus far and she underwent breathing surgery before being pulled up on her yard debut in 2m Huntingdon maiden last month; handicap debutante.
runners.comment: Had wind op before pulled up on yard debut in Huntingdon maiden latest; handicap debutant

races.race_name: Stables Business Park Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Puddlesinthepark
runners.spotlight: Five-time chase winne, the latest at Ffos Las (3m4f, good) off this mark in October; has been below par in three runs since but his usual cheekpieces are back on and he may appreciate this better ground.
runners.comment: Five-time chase winner who is back on his last winning mark

runners.name: Anytrixwilldo
runners.spotlight: His sole success came when landing this race last year (good to firm) in first-time cheekpieces off 3lb lower mark; has ran with credit since when placed and his second at Ludlow (3m, good to soft) in February will have set him up nicely for a repeat bid.
runners.comment: His sole success came when landing this race last year off 3lb lower mark.

runners.name: Wicked Thoughts
runners.spotlight: Made a winning chase debut at this track (2m7f, good to firm) in November off 1lb lower mark; put a poor run behind him when third of six back here (2m7f, good) last month and must come into consideration if he adapts to the step up in distance.
runners.comment: Won here in November and steps up in distance.

runners.name: Always Busy
runners.spotlight: Dual winning chaser who confirmed his well-being when second of four finishers at Southwell (3m, good) eight days ago; steps up in trip and if that brings about any improvement then he should be in the firing line.
runners.comment: Dual winning chaser who confirmed his well-being when second at Southwell eight days ago.

runners.name: Autonomous Cloud
runners.spotlight: Won three times over fences for Fergal O'Brien; beaten favourite on his stable debut at Doncaster (3m, good to soft) in February but has now dropped to a career-low mark.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite on stable debut at Doncaster in February but dropped to career-low mark

runners.name: Moroder
runners.spotlight: Six-time chase winner but now appears to be on the downgrade; pulled up when bidding to win the Grimthorpe at Doncaster for the third time in February and was tailed off at Ascot (2m5f, good) 11 days ago; can only be watched at present.
runners.comment: Six-time chase winner but now appears to be on the downgrade.

races.race_name: Stag Environmental Polaris Exmoor Devon Somerset Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Inferno Sacree
runners.spotlight: Showed improved form when stepping up to 3m to land his sixth success over hurdles, over C&D (good to soft) in February and followed up at Plumpton (3m1f, good to soft) last month; remains unexposed as a stayer and holds strong claims to land a three-timer.
runners.comment: Stepped up to this trip when successful over C&D in February and followed up last month.

runners.name: Al Sayah
runners.spotlight: All four wins over hurdles have came in the month of May; returned from her winter break when last of four at Plumpton (2m2f, good to soft) last month; should be sharper and steps up in trip but is 10lb above her last winning mark.
runners.comment: Returned from her winter break when last of four at Plumpton last month.

runners.name: Just A Rose
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut here (2m3f, good to soft) in January 2025 but has not gone on from that so far; pulled up on her last two starts, she steps up in trip with a tongue-tie and cheekpieces both on for the first time; still lightly raced but needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Made a winning debut here in January 2025 but has not gone on from that so far.
runners.owner: Mrs Jennifer Irish, Kate & Andrew Brooks

runners.name: Earth King
runners.spotlight: Has four wins over hurdles but is 2lb above his last winning mark; returns from a break and probably needs to come down a few pounds.
runners.comment: Has four wins over hurdles but is 2lb above his last winning mark.

races.race_name: Mike's Seventieth Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase

runners.name: Gata Ban
runners.spotlight: Second on her chase debut at Fakenham (2m5f, good to soft) in October but has failed to back up that run since; no improvement following wind surgery last time and has been dropped another 5lb.
runners.comment: Second on her chase debut at Fakenham in October but has failed to back up that run since.

runners.name: Hall Lane
runners.spotlight: Both wins when trained by Alan King; showed little on return from 404-day layoff at Leicester in January for new yard but much better when runner-up at Huntingdon (2m7f, good) last month; runs off the same mark and holds place claims at least.
runners.comment: Showed little on return in January but much better when second at Huntingdon last month.

runners.name: Illogical Logic
runners.spotlight: Just the one win in 22 starts but three decent runs when placed prior to a below par effort when tailed off at Plumpton (3m1f, good to soft) last month; his poor win record does not inspire.
runners.comment: Three decent runs prior to a below par effort when tailed off at Plumpton last month.

runners.name: School For Scandal
runners.spotlight: Has five wins over fences including one over C&D; below form on his last two starts but may show improvement on this better ground.
runners.comment: Below form on his last two starts but may show improvement on this better ground.
runners.owner: K Johns & Joe Tickle Racing Club

runners.name: Mcgregors Charge
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Perth (3m, good) last August off 9lb lower mark when trained by Ella Pickard; two decent efforts when placed subsequently but was pulled up at Hexham in October when last seen; makes stable debut and worth a look in the betting.
runners.comment: Won at Perth last August and two good runs when placed subsequently.

runners.name: Cave Article
runners.spotlight: Won for the third time over fences at Southwell (3m, good) last June off this mark; below that form in three runs subsequently but has age on his side and worth considering.
runners.comment: Won for the third time over fences at Southwell last June off this mark.

runners.name: Boys Of Wexford
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner who showed up well until tiring on his first start for three months when fourth of eight finishers at Hereford (2m5f, good) 15 days ago; first-time visor replaces regular cheekpieces; place claims.
runners.comment: Showed up well on his first start for three months when fourth at Hereford 15 days ago.

races.race_name: St Michael's Church Orchard Portman Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 3 Elimination) (GBB)

runners.name: Mac J
runners.spotlight: Beaten 23l and 30l on his two runs in bumpers and likely to need a trip when he goes hurdling.
runners.comment: Beaten 23l and 30l on his two runs in bumpers.

runners.name: Order Driven
runners.spotlight: Beaten 48l and 26l on his two runs in bumpers last month and needs more time.
runners.comment: Beaten 48l and 26l on his two runs in bumpers last month and needs more time.

runners.name: Kilifi Creek
runners.spotlight: 88,000euros 3yo; third foal; half-brother to point/useful bumper winner Bud Fox; dam 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner, sister to hurdle winners Our Roxane (2m) and Masked Phantom (2m4f), out of maiden half-sister to useful 2m-3m2f chase winner Song Sung Blue; yard is racking up the winners recently and must be taken seriously on this debut.
runners.comment: Yard is racking up the winners recently and must be taken seriously on this debut.

runners.name: Lost In The Dream
runners.spotlight: Twelfth foal; brother to three winners including Lord Leoso (6.5f-1m6f including 2yo/Listed; RPR 103) and Look At Me (German Listed-placed 6f 2yo/7f), half-brother to six winners including Lord Dubai (1m) and Legionar (bumper and 2m3f-2m5f hurdle); dam Italian 1m1f winner; stable in top form at present and no surprise were he to make a successful debut.
runners.comment: Stable in top form at present and no surprise were he to make a successful debut.

runners.name: Worksmartnothard
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-brother to winners James's Gate (useful bumper and 2m/2m3f hurdle), Black Lightning (2m4f/2m5f hurdle), Going Live (point/2m3f hurdle) and Doyannie (2m1f chase); dam 2m hurdle/2m4f chase winner; plenty of winners in his pedigree which augurs well for this debut.
runners.comment: Plenty of winners in his pedigree which augurs well for this debut.

runners.name: Jacks Brother
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam unplaced 6f-1m4f; unplaced in three points; stable 0-4 in bumpers over the last five years; rules debut and best to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Unplaced in three points; stable 0-4 in bumpers over the last five years.

runners.name: Roll A Dollar
runners.spotlight: 24,000euros 3yo; seventh foal; brother to winners Hit The Frame (3m1f chase) and Hard Stare (point), half-brother to useful 2m2f hurdle winner Cleatus Poolaw; dam 2m3f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner; second in a maiden point for Bradley Gibbs last month; makes stable debut and should do better when he goes hurdling over further.
runners.comment: Makes stable debut and should do better when he goes hurdling over further.

runners.name: Thunderous Sea
runners.spotlight: Beaten 69l on debut in bumper at Hereford (2m, good) last month and looks likely to need more time.
runners.comment: Beaten 69l on debut in bumper at Hereford last month and looks likely to need more time.

races.race_name: Country & Western Night 25th April Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: La Belle Forest
runners.spotlight: She has improved with each of her three starts, having a bit up her sleeve when winning over C&D four weeks ago (5f); makes her handicap debut with the potential for better.
runners.comment: Improved with each run and latest C&D win was a comfortable one; can do better

runners.name: Loleeta
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f fillies' novice a Lingfield (AW) last summer; sent off at big prices and has finished down the field in two handicap runs this year; arrives with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Down the field at big prices in two handicap runs this year; arrives with a point to prove

runners.name: Filly Foden
runners.spotlight: In good form for her new trainer early in the year, winning a C&D novice and following up in a Lingfield handicap one week later; two lesser efforts have followed and she may need some more help from the assessor.
runners.comment: Won twice over the winter, including C&D, but handicapper in charge more recently

runners.name: Alfa Duplicate
runners.spotlight: Improved for the drop to 5f when 1.5l too strong for Call Margot over C&D in February; defied a 7lb rise when narrowly winning a 6f handicap at Newcastle 19 days ago, form that was given a handsome boost when the runner-up won at Lingfield on Good Friday; another 4lb higher today but there could well be more in the tank and her attitude will continue to stand her in good stead.
runners.comment: Chasing a hat-trick after wins over C&D and at Newcastle (6f); likely there's more to come

runners.name: Call Margot
runners.spotlight: Beaten 1.5l by Alfa Duplicate over C&D in February and reopposes on 11lb better terms today; could do with settling better but she can't be discounted.
runners.comment: 11lb better off with Alfa Duplicate for 1.5l on their C&D run in February; each-way shout

runners.name: Naana's Shadow
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice over C&D before winning a 5f novice at Wolverhampton in October (when trained by Alice Haynes; picked up for 20,000gns by new connections one month later); remains open to further progress but the absence of her usual tongue tie sounds a note of caution.
runners.comment: Progressive for Alice Haynes prior to changing hands in November; tongue tie absent today

runners.name: Artista
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 5f as a 2yo, including an AW nursery off 2lb lower; she needs to better last month's two runs but this is a drop in class so it isn't out of the question.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins as a 2yo; not at her best twice last month but now drops in class

runners.name: Queensmaite
runners.spotlight: Third of nine in the C&D maiden on this card 12 months ago; hasn't built on it in three subsequent runs but she isn't fully exposed and a bigger effort is likely to be forthcoming at some point soon.
runners.comment: Promising debut on this card 12 months ago; no progress since but still early days

races.race_name: GB Civil Engineering Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Vinciamo
runners.spotlight: Promising debut for Marcus Tregoning one year ago; absent 11 months prior to his stable debut four weeks ago, a race where he raced too freely and weakened in the final furlong (10-11 favourite); should last longer this time and he is a leading contender.
runners.comment: Too free on stable debut (5f, AW) last month after 11 months off; still capable of better

runners.name: Ay Up Duck
runners.spotlight: Encouraging debut when chasing home the red-hot favourite in a 7f maiden here 12 days ago; that race was slowly run to halfway and the form doesn't look convincing; not bred to be a 5f horse either.
runners.comment: Encouraging 2nd on debut here (7f) 12 days ago but the form may not be solid; drops to 5f

runners.name: Shes Got A Brother
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns foal, 100,000gns breeze-up 2yo; seventh foal; half-sister to winners Future History (French/Australian 1m1f-1m6f including Group 3; RPR 119) and Paddy's Fancy (1m7f hurdle); dam 1m 2yo winner (87); hood and tongue tie enlisted for debut but she is starting out in a winnable race; betting to guide.
runners.comment: 100,000gns breeze-up 2yo; half-sister to Group 3 winner out of 1m 2yo winner; check market

runners.name: Likeadyinwasp
runners.spotlight: 9,000gns yearling; fourth foal; closely related to winners Cheeky Stanley (6f AW; RPR 62) and Karelen (Greek 6f); dam Italian 5.5f/6f winner; likely best watched on debut.
runners.comment: 9,000gns close relation to two minor winners; likely best watched on debut

runners.name: Dolo's Star
runners.spotlight: 22-1 when last of five (Vinciamo 7.75l in front of her in third) on her Newcastle debut four weeks ago; a big step forward is required.
runners.comment: Nearly 8l behind Vinciamo on last month's Newcastle debut; big step forward required

runners.name: Aleppo Pepper
runners.spotlight: Well-beaten 66-1 shot on her 2yo debut; much more encouragement when second of 12 in a 5f Dundalk maiden on sole 3yo outing; that was 435 days ago and it is hard to know quite what to expect on her first run since leaving Kieran Cotter.
runners.comment: Second of 12 in a Dundalk maiden (5f, AW) 435 days ago; market useful on stable debut

races.race_name: Book The Bistro In Sherwood Restaurant Handicap

runners.name: Fidelius
runners.spotlight: Three AW wins since November, the latest a comfortable success at Lingfileld; held by Spring Is Sprung on C&D form from January but he should give his running again.
runners.comment: Enjoyed a good winter, winning three times; held by Spring Is Sprung on C&D run in January

runners.name: Master Of My Fate
runners.spotlight: Game effort when winning over C&D in February (his third C&D success) but he hasn't been at quite the same level twice since; this looks a warm race for the grade and he may come up short once again.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner, the latest in February; not quite so good twice since
runners.owner: Ms Janelle Cao, Ross Harmon & Partner

runners.name: Diomed Spirit
runners.spotlight: Successful in a Class 5 handicap at Lingfield in November off a 1lb lower mark; not at his best on his last two starts and may prove vulnerable again.
runners.comment: Needs to bounce back from two lesser runs; last win came in Class 5 company

runners.name: Spring Is Sprung
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; enjoyed a purple patch last summer, landing a big payday when scoring at Haydock in August; got the better of Fidelius over C&D on his return to action in January and he meets his old rival on 1lb better terms today; only fourth in a higher grade last time but he can make a bold bid back into 0-85 company.
runners.comment: C&D win in January; only 4th in a stronger race last month; bold bid expected

runners.name: Far Above The Law
runners.spotlight: His two wins for Alice Haynes came over 6f but he wasn't beaten far dropped to 5f when last seen in October; sold for 40,000gns soon afterwards; on a handy mark ahead of his stable debut; goes without headgear; betting should guide.
runners.comment: Of interest on best form for Alice Haynes; sold 40,000gns in October; betting to guide

runners.name: Marty Hopkirk
runners.spotlight: AW novice winner; good second at Doncaster on his handicap debut in July (5f, good to firm) but he came up short in hot contests twice after; still has low mileage and interesting back down in class after a layoff.
runners.comment: Struggled in good handicaps on final 2 runs in 2025; low mileage; down in class for return

runners.name: Hiya Maite
runners.spotlight: No win since June 2024 but several good runs, including over C&D, since then; conditions suit and no obvious reason why he wouldn't be in the thick of it again.
runners.comment: On a losing run but well suited by C&D and should be in the thick of it once again

races.race_name: Come Racing For Free 18th June Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Got The Booty
runners.spotlight: Two runs last year, making the running on each occasion and showing better form when second of ten at Kempton on the second occasion; that form has a solid look to it and she is open to further progress; key player with Oisin Murphy booked.
runners.comment: Front-running second at Kempton when last seen (form franked); more to come this year

runners.name: Polly Darling
runners.spotlight: Sent off the 8-13 favourite for her Thirsk debut (5f, good) 362 days ago and after running green when initially asked for her effort, she kept on well for a comfortable success; subsequent absence suggests it hasn't been plain sailing since but as a well-bred unbeaten filly from a leading yard she commands plenty of respect.
runners.comment: Overcame inexperience to record cosy win at Thirsk 362 days ago; respected despite absence
runners.owner: Mrs Polly Gibson & Miss Pollyanne Gibson

runners.name: Button It
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two 5f AW runs last month; the extra furlong is not enough to tempt today.
runners.comment: Modest form in two 5f AW runs last month; the extra furlong is not enough to tempt today

runners.name: Jumeirah Sands
runners.spotlight: Didn't live up to market expectations at Wolverhampton (6f, AW; 7-2) last August but she was always on the back foot after missing the break; interesting to see if her backers return.
runners.comment: Well backed but never featured after slow start on last August's debut (6f, AW); unexposed

runners.name: Hever Beauty
runners.spotlight: 200-1 when a well-beaten last of 11 on her debut at Chelmsford (6f) two weeks ago.
runners.comment: 200-1 when a well-beaten last of 11 on her debut at Chelmsford (6f) two weeks ago

runners.name: Salamis Bay
runners.spotlight: Stayed on in the final furlong having been outpaced (and ran green) 2f out on Lingfield debut ten weeks ago (6f); that run gives her something to build on but her pedigree points to longer trips suiting better in time.
runners.comment: Debut effort was promising but she may not come into her own until tackling 7f+

runners.name: Cotai Starlight
runners.spotlight: Finished in midfield at Newcastle in her one start for Ed Walker as a 2yo (RPR 56); picked up by new connections for 7,000gns last October; pedigree offers hope of much better at some point.
runners.comment: Midfield finish in one run for Ed Walker last year; bred to do better at some point

races.race_name: Conference & Events At Southwell Racecourse Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Senor Cortez
runners.spotlight: Scored twice (1m6f/2m) for Darryll Holland last summer and he made an encouraging start for his new yard when third of seven in 2m Lingfield handicap 20 days ago; remains quite lightly raced and likely better to come.
runners.comment: Encouraging start for new yard when third at Lingfield 20 days ago; likely better to come

runners.name: Carlton
runners.spotlight: Thriving at present and he followed up 1m6f Chelmsford success in February with an emphatic victory over hurdles at Huntingdon last month; big shout once more.
runners.comment: Thriving and won at Chelmsford before emphatic Huntingdon hurdles victory; big shout

runners.name: Peaky Blinder
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage son of Soldier Hollow who advanced his form when landing 1m6f handicap at Redcar in October; off since (gelded) but this longer trip promises to suit and yard continues to go very well.
runners.comment: Scored at Redcar in October; this longer trip promises to suit and yard is going very well

runners.name: Adjuvant
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2023 and he signed off for last term with a below-par 14th in November Handicap at Doncaster in November; yard is going well though so no forlorn hope off a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Winless since 2023; yard going well though so no forlorn hope off a reduced mark

runners.name: Imperial Sovereign
runners.spotlight: On a lengthy losing sequence but he ended 2025 in very good form on the AW, third in 1m6f Wolverhampton handicap in November; possibilities.
runners.comment: On a lengthy losing sequence but he ended 2025 in very good form on the AW; possibilities

runners.name: Rock N Roll Pinkie
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a good 2025 when a three-time winner (from 1m6f to 2m) but she came in last in 1m6f Nottingham handicap on her final run in October; has gone well off a break, though.
runners.comment: Enjoyed a good 2025 when a three-time winner; has gone well off a break too

races.race_name: Ladies Day Sunday 16th August Handicap

runners.name: Down To The Kid
runners.spotlight: Took this in 2025 and he comes here better than ever, bagging a third course win of 2026 in February; posted a very good second in another 7f handicap here last time so rates a big player off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Took this in 2025 and better than ever in 2026; rates a big player in his follow-up bid

runners.name: Tilted Kilt
runners.spotlight: Winless in 2025 and also didn't prove the easiest to catch right; has gone very well off a break though so not dismissed off a handy-looking mark.
runners.comment: Winless in 2025; has gone very well off a break so not dismissed off a handy-looking mark

runners.name: Shimmering Spin
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Chelmsford (1m) in September but he's been off since turning in a poor sixth there in December; more is required despite a slipping mark.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Chelmsford (1m) in September but off since a poor sixth there in December

runners.name: Silver Trumpet
runners.spotlight: Scored at Epsom (7f) last August and proved largely reliable in 2025; this course scorer goes well fresh too so not out of things.
runners.comment: Largely reliable in 2025 and this course scorer goes well fresh too; not out of things

runners.name: Eldrickjones
runners.spotlight: Scored at Newcastle (7f) in December and in good nick until only sixth back there (7f) 35 days ago; the sort to bounce back though.
runners.comment: Scored at Newcastle in December and in good nick until only sixth back there 35 days ago

runners.name: Gunlock
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start for Martin Dunne at Kempton (7f) in December and not disgraced when eighth here (6f) following month; starts out for another new yard having changed hands for 10,000gns since; hood refitted.
runners.comment: Solid eighth here latest; begins for new yard having changed hands for 10,000gns since

races.race_name: Summer Socials At Southwell Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Akrivos
runners.spotlight: Is 0-10 but he arrives in decent nick, a close eighth in 7f handicap here two weeks ago; possibilities from a slipping mark with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Is 0-10 but arrives in decent nick; possibilities from slipping mark with cheekpieces on

runners.name: Electrocution
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Wolverhampton (7f) in February and has continued in good form, third of 11 in 7f Chelmsford handicap four weeks ago; in the mix again stepped up to 1m.
runners.comment: Good third of 11 in Chelmsford handicap four weeks ago; can make his presence felt again

runners.name: Captain Bruce
runners.spotlight: A dual AW scorer at Chelmsford (7f) and Newcastle (1m2f) last autumn; tongue tied when failing to beat a rival on her seasonal return though in 1m Newcastle handicap 19 days ago so has something to prove.
runners.comment: Failed to beat rival on return in Newcastle h'cap 19 days ago so has something to prove

runners.name: Hazy Cosmic Jane
runners.spotlight: Made the frame five times as a juvenile (from 6f to 1m) but she came in last of ten on her return in 8.5f Wolverhampton handicap 24 days ago; cheekpieces go on now.
runners.comment: Last of ten on her return in 8.5f Wolverhampton handicap 24 days ago; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Musical Soldier
runners.spotlight: Has failed to make the frame in his six outings to date, only seventh of nine in 8.5f Wolverhampton handicap 16 days ago; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Has failed to make the frame in his six outings to date; others are preferred

runners.name: Shes Got The Blues
runners.spotlight: Not discredited when fifth of nine in 9.5f Wolverhampton handicap 45 days ago but more is needed if she's to get off the mark at the 11th attempt here.
runners.comment: Respectable Wolverhampton fifth latest; more needed to score at the 11th attempt here

runners.name: City Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Son of Cotai Glory who took a step forward on his first go in handicaps when fourth of 11 at Wolverhampton (8.5f) last month, leading until over 1f out; he must enter calculations for his in-form handler.
runners.comment: Improved when fourth at Wolverhampton latest; must enter calculations for in-form handler

runners.name: Mohmentous
runners.spotlight: Son of Mohaather who has shown only poor form in his three outings, hooded when a never-dangerous fourth of five in 6f Kempton maiden 50 days ago; gelded ahead of his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Hooded when fourth of five in Kempton maiden 50 days ago; gelded ahead of handicap debut

runners.name: Lady Of Clover
runners.spotlight: Has improved with each of her three starts for her current yard, posting an excellent third of nine on her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (8.5f) 16 days ago; she can go well again off an unchanged mark in a refitted hood.
runners.comment: Excellent third at Wolverhampton latest; can go well off unchanged mark in a refitted hood

runners.name: Nicolai
runners.spotlight: Just poor form shown to date, visored for his handicap debut when a fair sixth of 11 at Wolverhampton (8.5f) last month; more is required from this son of Sergei Prokofiev.
runners.comment: Visored when sixth at Wolverhampton last month; more is required
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Racing 1 & Andrew Bengough

runners.name: Harswell River
runners.spotlight: Opened her account at Redcar (1m) in September but failed to go on in four subsequent runs, only sixth in 1m Newcastle nursery in December; she needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Opened her account at Redcar (1m) in September but failed to go on in four subsequent runs

runners.name: Dash Of Class
runners.spotlight: Advanced her form on her handicap debut when fourth of eight at Wolverhampton (9.5f) 27 days ago, despite going very freely; this daughter of Earthlight looks capable of better still, especially if more tractable in a first-time hood; likely player.
runners.comment: Raced very freely when good fourth at Wolverhampton latest; likely player now in a hood

runners.name: What Fools Believe
runners.spotlight: Zoustar filly who has cut little ice in her four runs to date, last of seven on her handicap debut at Newcastle (7f) 49 days ago; lots more is required.
runners.comment: Zoustar filly who has cut little ice in her four runs to date; lots more is required

runners.name: Violet Goldsmith
runners.spotlight: Daughter of Oasis Dream who finished well held on all her three starts at 2yrs, seventh of ten in 7f Kempton novice in October; cheekpieces go on for her return/handicap debut and this half-sister to the stable's smart miler Accidental Agent could still do better.
runners.comment: Well held all three runs at 2yrs; cheekpieces on for h'cap debut and could still do better

races.race_name: Follow Gowran Park On Insta (C & G) Maiden

runners.name: Gatlinburg
runners.spotlight: Churchill colt was slowly away and never on terms in Naas maiden last September and again not the best of breaks on last month's Dundalk return, running green and weakening into midfield; may need a bit more time.
runners.comment: Green on last month's return at Dundalk; may need more time
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing & Mrs A M O'Brien

runners.name: Suir Monad
runners.spotlight: Dark Angel gelding was a 38,000euros yearling; half-brother to four US winners inc Candy Flower (Graded-placed 8.5f turf; RPR 104) and Phantom Vision (5f AW/turf inc 2yo; 97); dam US 1m/8.3f dirt stakes winner; solid debut fourth (third won next time out) over this trip at Dundalk in December; yard in hot form and looks a big player, providing he handles the soft ground.
runners.comment: Dundalk debut effort gives him leading claims and yard in hot form too

runners.name: Stellar View
runners.spotlight: Starman gelding a 11,500euros yearling; fourth foal; dam 6f 2yo winner (RPR 88), half-sister to 1m4f-2m1f winner Mondain, out of unraced sister to 1m-1m2f Group 1 winner Manduro; showed some degree of promise on Dundalk debut in November over this trip; no surprise if he improved enough to play a major role in this weak affair.
runners.comment: Some degree of promise on Dundalk debut; could improve enough to play a major role

runners.name: Mount Eden
runners.spotlight: Didn't count on both Dundalk runs over the winter, midfield each time; nicely-bred type, potential improver switched to turf.
runners.comment: Didn't count on both Dundalk runs over the winter; potential improver switched to turf
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Sir Aston
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation colt cost 55,000euros as a foal, £68,000 as a yearling and 56,000gns at the breeze-ups; first foal; dam French 1m5f winner, out of unraced half-sister to Yorkshire Oaks winner Shareta; market best guide on debut in what looks a weak maiden.
runners.comment: 56,000gns breeze-up buy; market best guide on debut in what looks a weak maiden

runners.name: Nicky Larson
runners.spotlight: Never better than midfield on Tipperary debut last August, showing up more prominently on last month's Naas return before fading; may be more one for a handicap.
runners.comment: Modest enough efforts so far, may be more one for a handicap

runners.name: Lunar Tide
runners.spotlight: Sea The Moon gelding a 70,000gns foal; half-brother to 7f AW/1m winner Cavalo Nero (RPR 91); dam Listed-placed 7.4f 2yo winner (96); never landed a meaningful blow on Dundalk debut in February; back in trip here.
runners.comment: Never landed a meaningful blow on Dundalk debut in February; back in trip here

runners.name: Mighty Wave
runners.spotlight: Closely related to winners Young Ireland (Group-placed 1m2f) and Feile Na Mban (1m4f), half-brother to winner Ceol Na Nog; dam 1m winner (89), half-sister to Irish Derby winner Soldier Of Fortune; never counted on recent Curragh debut when well held in testing conditions; better for the experience and drier ground may suit.
runners.comment: Better for recent debut run and drier ground may suit

runners.name: Only One Scobie
runners.spotlight: Plum last on all three Dundalk runs over the winter and beat only one rival on turf debut at Navan last month; easily ruled out.
runners.comment: Poor form, easily ruled out

runners.name: Point Cartwright
runners.spotlight: Couple of poor efforts on AW over the winter, can only be watched now switched to turf.
runners.comment: Couple of poor efforts on AW over the winter, can only be watched now switched to turf

races.race_name: Societies Welcome At Gowran Park Handicap

runners.name: Master Garvey
runners.spotlight: Has done a lot of racing since his Dundalk win just over a year ago, one of only two wins in 47-race career; solid third in 1m Dundalk handicap eight days ago; player back on turf if he can build on that.
runners.comment: Dundalk win just over a year ago, one of only two wins in 47-race career; chance at best
runners.owner: Patrick M O'Brien/Larry Murray/Richard W Walsh/Derek Cummins

runners.name: Miss Americana
runners.spotlight: Maiden ran some respectable races in handicaps last year; rounded off last term with a lesser effort when sixth behind Hell Left Loose over C&D; each-way claims with Shane Foley booked on return.
runners.comment: Work to do to reverse form with Hell Left Loose but player at her best; good jockey

runners.name: Carrickfinn
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 20 starts but career-best effort was when runner-up over C&D last June, albeit on good ground; only fair efforts on AW of late; others are more likely; 2lb wrong.
runners.comment: Maiden after 20 starts but best effort was when runner-up over C&D in June, albeit on good

runners.name: Not Simple
runners.spotlight: Won Cork handicap (7f, soft) last September; failed to sustain that progress in four subsequent outings, including on return at the Curragh 12 days ago; still 6lb above her winning mark.
runners.comment: Won Cork handicap (7f, soft) last September; failed to sustain that progress since

runners.name: Rock Basher
runners.spotlight: Winless from 20 starts; claimed for 5,000euros after much-improved neck defeat in 1m Dundalk claimer in October; failed to make an impact in four runs for Denis Hogan; new yard with good 7lb claimer aboard; market will guide.
runners.comment: Winless from 20 starts; new yard with good 7lb claimer aboard; market will guide

runners.name: Kilgharrahs Love
