runners.spotlight: Made the frame in two nurseries in 2024; form deteriorated last year after a fair fourth in early-season 7f course handicap; well beaten on Curragh return; cheekpieces are added now.
runners.comment: Ran well here over 7f last year but form has deteriorated since; cheekpieces on now

runners.name: Lady Arwen
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner off much higher marks but on a long losing run since 2023; well beaten at Dundalk first two starts this year; contender if at her best returning to turf.
runners.comment: Dual course winner off much higher marks but on a long losing run since 2023

runners.name: Red Veil
runners.spotlight: Out of her depth in maiden hurdles over the winter; finished third in C&D claimer on final run for Michael O'Callaghan last September but regressive since.
runners.comment: Finished third in C&D claimer on final run for Michael O'Callaghan but regressive since

runners.name: Hell Left Loose
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner on turf at 6f-1m, including when bolting up over C&D under this rider in October from 7lb lower; showed a return to form last two outings at the Curragh and will be dangerous if allowed to dictate matters.
runners.comment: Bolted up over C&D in October from 7lb lower; dangerous if allowed to dictate matters

runners.name: The Blue Panther
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; added his fifth career win with with heavy-ground Listowel success over 7f in September; rounded off last term with fair fifth at Leopardstown after slow break; goes well fresh.
runners.comment: C&D winner; fair fifth at Leopardstown after slow break last time; goes well fresh
runners.owner: Ms Julianne McNally & Colette McNally & D McNally

runners.name: Bang Po
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground winner in September 2023 but no joy since; failed to beat a rival home at Dundalk when last seen; safe to rule out; 5lb wrong.
runners.comment: Heavy-ground winner in September 2023 but no joy since; safe to rule out

runners.name: Roderick
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner on turf who wasn't beaten far in pair of C&D handicaps last season; no impression in two winter AW spins; capable of getting involved back on turf; handles underfoot conditions.
runners.comment: Five-time winner on turf who wasn't beaten far in pair of C&D handicaps last season

runners.name: Rock Etoile
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner for former yard (won this race in 2023) and has an excellent overall record at the track (form figures 2027134212); four poor AW runs for this yard but a return to this venue could spark a revival from a basement mark.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner (won this race in 2023) and has an excellent overall record at the track
runners.owner: Conor McKenna & Patrick J McKenna & Emmet McKenna

runners.name: Zephron
runners.spotlight: Three-time Curragh winner; five wins in all, none since April 2024; weak form last season; heavily punted at Navan (6f) on return/stable debut 10 days ago but was always behind and finished last.
runners.comment: Heavily punted at Navan on return/stable debut 10 days ago but was always behind

runners.name: Famous Enough
runners.spotlight: Cork/AW winner over 1m; close fourth over C&D (good to yielding) in September from 3lb lower; got going too late when beaten favourite at Cork end of last term; ground may be too slow for him on return.
runners.comment: Cork/AW winner over 1m; close fourth over C&D in September; ground may be too slow

runners.name: She's Ideal
runners.spotlight: Showed a bit in maidens on better ground at two; ground was slow for her heavy defeats in turf handicaps; fair effort in Dundalk claimer in October; improvement needed but Colin Keane is an eyecatching booking on return.
runners.comment: Didn't handle soft ground previously but interesting that Colin Keane has been booked

runners.name: Galactic Grey
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose performer is 0-19 and hasn't been seen for more than three years; best watched.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose performer is 0-19 and hasn't been seen in more than three years; best watched

races.race_name: Irish Injured Jockeys Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Feel The Love
runners.spotlight: Half-sister by St Mark's Basilica to eight winners, including the top-class Fancy Blue (Group 1 Prix de Diane and Nassau Stakes), Smuggler's Cove (Listed) and Casterton (Listed); dam 7f 2yo winner, sister to Derby winner High Chaparral; encouraging enough Curragh debut last month, weakening late on when third in 7f fillies' maiden; bred to improve for this step up in trip.
runners.comment: Encouraging enough Curragh debut last month; bred to improve for this step up in trip
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Sounds Great
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam Barrington Court won two 1m4f Listed races; promising debut run in end of season Galway fillies' maiden when staying-on fourth over 8.5f; bred to relish mid-distances this year.
runners.comment: Encouraging Galway debut; could improve more than most over mid-distances this year

runners.name: Cape Primrose
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica daughter of Irish 1,000 Guineas heroine Again; better of two late-season juvenile outings on second occasion when (odds-on) up with pace throughout and beaten 0.5l in Thurles fillies' maiden over 1m; good chance she'll stay 1m2f this year (Irish Oaks-entered) and shortlisted.
runners.comment: Narrow Thurles defeat in October; should stay 1m2f and definite contender
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: De Vega Queen
runners.spotlight: Lope De Vega filly cost 38,000euros as a foal and 45,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to winners Succeedandsurpass (US/Canadian Graded stakes-placed 1m turf/8.5f AW; RPR 110), Gaasy (KSA 1m2f) and Gordons Aura (2m hurdle); dam 1m 2yo/1m2f AW winner (89), out of Fillies' Mile winner; yard have made a decent start to the season and market support on debut should be noted.
runners.comment: Yard have made a decent start to the season and market support on debut should be noted

runners.name: Rebel Moon
runners.spotlight: Lope De Vega filly cost 500,000gns as a yearling; closely related to French winners Alzire (1m inc Listed; 105) and Pemba Bay (7f/1m inc Listed; 104), half-sister to three winners inc Tasman Bay (Group-placed 1m AW/1m2f turf; 113); dam well-related 1m2f/12.5f winner; one of two for the yard, market likely informative on debut.
runners.comment: Lope De Vega filly a 500,000gns yearling; one of two for the yard, market informative

runners.name: Chestnut Palace
runners.spotlight: Palace Pier filly was a 145,000euros foal; half-sister to five Flat winners inc Fighting Irish (6f 2yo inc Group 2; RPR 106), Vulcan (1m-1m2f inc AW; 96) and Wide With Delight (7f; 88); dam unraced sister to 1m Group 1 winner Virtual; eyecatching debut at Naas last month when never-nearer fifth of 10 under considerate enough handling; one to consider now upped in trip.
runners.comment: Eyecatching debut at Naas last month; one to consider now upped in trip

runners.name: Johanna Walsh
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars filly a 460,000euros yearling, is sister to winners Kildare Legend (1m3f) and Golden Horse (1m), half-sister to French 6.5f AW winner My Runner; dam half-sister to 1m1f Group 1 winner Mekhtaal; plenty green on debut at Thurles in October but shaped with nice bit of promise when staying-on third (1.25l off runner-up Cape Primrose) over 1m; should relish this longer trip and could get a lot closer to Cape Primrose now.
runners.comment: Plenty green on Thurles debut; could well improve enough to play major role

races.race_name: Gowran Park Racing Club (C & G) Maiden

runners.name: Power Strike
runners.spotlight: Two runs this year haven't offered much encouragement; tongue-tie now fitted but yard have likely stronger contenders.
runners.comment: Runs this year haven't offered much encouragement; yard have likely stronger contenders

runners.name: Casamorati
runners.spotlight: Starspangledbanner colt is first foal of a 7f AW winner (RPR 84), a half-sister to 1m2f winner Dive For Gold, out of close relation to US 1m1f Grade 1 winner Secret Status; market will guide on debut.
runners.comment: Starspangledbanner colt is first foal of a 7f AW winner; market will guide on debut

runners.name: Diligo
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt cost 200,000gns as a yearling; half-brother to winners Interpretation (1m2f-1m6f inc Australian Group 3; RPR 112), Bahamian Club (US 1m/8.5f turf; 97) and Sir Laurence Graff (1m2f AW inc 2yo; 85); dam twice-raced half-sister to UAE 1m4f Group 1 winner Dolniya; one of three for the yard, market best guide on debut.
runners.comment: Wootton Bassett colt cost 200,000gns as a yearling; market best guide on debut
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing & AMO Racing Limited

runners.name: Goodmenaremissed
runners.spotlight: Awtaad gelding a 4,000euros foal and 16,000euros yearling; half-brother to 1m2f-1m4f winner Night Bear (inc AW; RPR 85); dam poor maiden half-sister to Listed winners Les Fazzani (1m2f-1m4f) and Miss Lips (German 5f); unlikely one to make an immediate impact.
runners.comment: 16,000gns yearling; unlikely one to make an immediate impact

runners.name: Kepler
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt is second foal; brother to 7f 2yo-10.3f winner Whirl (inc Group 1s/Oaks second; RPR 123); dam 1m winner (81), sister to Group 1 winners Hydrangea (1m/1m4f), The United States (Australian 1m2f) and Hermosa (1,000 Guineas/Irish 1,000 Guineas); top pedigree but doesn't hold any fancy entries.
runners.comment: Brother to Whirl, from top family; doesn't hold any fancy entries
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Rebel Ascendant
runners.spotlight: Nathaniel gelding a 40,000gns yearling; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Mister Universe (7f Listed) and Knight Owl (useful 1m-1m1f); market strength worth noting on debut.
runners.comment: Nathaniel gelding a 40,000gns yearling; market strength worth noting on debut

runners.name: Reynir
runners.spotlight: Siyouni colt is second foal; half-brother to last year's 1m2f winner Reyenzi (RPR 108); dam maiden half-sister to winners Raydara (7f 2yo Group 2), Rondonia (1m/1m2f) and Rayna (1m3f/1m4f); nicely-bred debutant, has to be watched for market support.
runners.comment: Half-brother to last year's 1m2f winner Reyenzi; market best guide on debut
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Starflow
runners.spotlight: Starman gelding; 18,000euros yearling; half-brother to winners Lesson Learned (1m AW 2yo), Loterie (US 1m turf) and Marcolini (Spanish 1m/1m1f); dam unraced half-sister to Australian 7f/1m Listed winner Skiddaw Peak; 25-1, never sighted on Dundalk debut in November; probably best watched on return.
runners.comment: Never sighted on Dundalk debut in November; probably best watched on return

races.race_name: Thomastown Maiden

runners.name: Game Point
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in five consecutive starts for Ger Lyons at one stage; Colin Keane was booked for stable debut when never a factor at Naas last month; tongue-tied.
runners.comment: Some good form for Ger Lyons; never a factor on stable debut; tongue-tied

runners.name: Wild Sapphire
runners.spotlight: Very promising Curragh debut in 2024 when 0.5l behind subsequent Dewhurst runner-up Expanded; beaten 1l in 1m course maiden on this card last year on seasonal return; runner-up on return at Dundalk 13 days ago after being gelded; should go close in first-time visor.
runners.comment: Has some hot maiden form in the book; second on this card last year; should go close

runners.name: Mano Chicago
runners.spotlight: Third in 7f course maiden on debut before narrowly denied at Naas; gelded after last of five in Leopardstown Group 3 in July 2024; creditable fifth in Goffs Million that September; that's the strongest form on offer; the one to beat if he's ready after a lengthy absence.
runners.comment: Fifth in Goffs Millions; off the track a long time but the one to beat if he is fit
runners.owner: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit

runners.name: Take Me
runners.spotlight: Solid maiden form last year, the best of those a strong-finishing fifth at Navan over 1m2f (good); rounded off the season with a good fourth at Leopardstown (1m1f, heavy); shortlisted on return with Colin Keane aboard again for red-hot yard.
runners.comment: Some solid maiden form last year; shortlisted on return with Keane on for red-hot yard

runners.name: Tears I Nevercried
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unplaced 1m-1m2f in France, closely related to 6f winner L'Invincible; can only be watched.
runners.comment: Dam unplaced 1m-1m2f in France, closely related to 6f winner L'Invincible; watch

runners.name: An Cailleach
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister to winners Echo Brava (1m-1m6f inc AW; RPR 87), Mitigator (1m-1m3f inc AW; 76) and Junoesque (prolific 1m-1m4f inc AW; 73); dam 7.4f-1m4f winner (inc 2yo; 66), out of 6f-1m winner; best watched.
runners.comment: Sixth foal; half-sister to several winners but can only be watched on debut

runners.name: Catherine Magennis
runners.spotlight: Saxon Warrior filly; half-sister to six winners inc Earl Of Tyrone (1m4f-2m inc AW/Listed; RPR 112) and Countess Of Tyrone (1m4f/2m inc Listed; 95); unraced dam out of 1m2f Group 1 winner; plenty to like on paper.
runners.comment: Half-sister to six winners inc classy Earl Of Tyrone and Countess Of Tyrone; lots to like

runners.name: Tommy Go Maith
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in maiden hurdle for former yard; pulled up in Navan bumper on yard debut in February; tongue-tied; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Pulled up in a maiden hurdle and bumper; safe to rule out despite tongue-tie

runners.name: Cracking Cloud
runners.spotlight: Cost 22,000gns as a yearling; half-brother to winners Jolly Jack Tar (1m2f AW; RPR 98), Act Fast (1m/1m1f; 81) and First Snowfall (1m2f AW; 67); dam 1m2f-1m4f winner (inc Group 2 Ribblesdale; 113); best watched.
runners.comment: Cost 22,000gns as a yearling; half-brother to several winners; best watched

runners.name: Musical Master
runners.spotlight: Mastercraftsman gelding cost 12,000euros as a yearling; half-brother to US 7.5f turf winner Goodfella; dam 7f winner; others preferred.
runners.comment: Mastercraftsman gelding cost 12,000euros; half-brother to US 7.5f turf winner Goodfella

runners.name: Sron Na Caise
runners.spotlight: Third foal; brother to 1m winner Electric Lightning (RPR 65); dam 6f turf 2yo/7f AW winner (RPR 91), out of maiden half-sister to useful winners Dare To Achieve (1m4f) and Ooty Hill (7f 2yo); market can guide.
runners.comment: Brother to 1m winner Electric Lightning (RPR 65); dam 6f turf 2yo/7f AW winner (RPR 91)

runners.name: Western Bandit
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-brother to 6f-1m winner In Haste (inc 2yo/Italy; RPR 78); dam 8.4f 2yo winner (78), sister to French 6.5f winner So Oops, half-sister to Group-placed 7f 2yo winner Takrice, out of 7f-1m1f winner; may need time.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 6f-1m winner In Haste (inc 2yo/Italy); dam 8.4f 2yo winner; may need time

runners.name: Eternal Echo
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner and third at Clonmel (9.5f) on sole Flat start for Gordon Elliott; struggled in two handicap hurdles since joining this yard; can only be watched at present.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who ran well on Flat for Gordon Elliott; two poor hurdle runs for this yard

runners.name: Massarat
runners.spotlight: Very expensive yearling was cheaply bought at three; tailed off last of six in 1m Dundalk conditions race last month.
runners.comment: Cheaply bought at three; tailed off last of six in 1m Dundalk conditions race last month

runners.name: Eagle's Quest
runners.spotlight: Recent maiden hurdle winner; midfield over 1m4f on first Flat start; 1m looked inadequate when staying on late to take ninth of 25 at the Curragh last month; could improve up in trip.
runners.comment: Hurdle winner; ninth of 25 at the Curragh over 1m last month; could improve up in trip
runners.owner: Jackie Cullen & A D Mullins & Ms Margaret Mullins

runners.name: Purple Moon
runners.spotlight: Ribchester gelding was tailed off when 50-1 for his 1m Curragh debut last month; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Tailed off when 50-1 for his 1m Curragh debut last month; hard to fancy

runners.name: Zammawar
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in three maidens over varying trips last summer; will be one for handicaps in due course.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in three maidens over varying trips; will be one for handicaps

races.race_name: Racing Again April 22nd Handicap

runners.name: I've Been Dreaming
runners.spotlight: Nursery winner on heavy ground at Galway in 2023; missed the 2024 season; very hard to fancy judged on three efforts last season, finishing last of 13 over C&D on latest.
runners.comment: Very hard to fancy judged on three efforts last season, finishing last over C&D on latest

runners.name: Ethelwulf
runners.spotlight: Had one good run for the Gosdens, namely a Yarmouth second; hard to fancy judged on his three Irish efforts at Dundalk but perhaps tackling turf can help.
runners.comment: Yarmouth second for Gosdens; hard to fancy judged on his three Irish efforts at Dundalk

runners.name: Khormor Syd
runners.spotlight: Never better than sixth in seven Flat outings; good hurdle debut effort when sixth of 21 to The Reverend at Cork in January; difficult to assess returning to the Flat.
runners.comment: Sixth of 21 to The Reverend at Cork in January; difficult to assess returning to the Flat

runners.name: Magna Gee Gee
runners.spotlight: AW winner in Britain (9.5f); okay form for current yard on AW at end of last year; handicapper giving him every chance returning to turf but best form is on a sound surface.
runners.comment: Handicapper giving him every chance returning to turf but best form is on a sound surface

runners.name: Skib Gold
runners.spotlight: Shock 80-1 C&D handicap winner on good last July off 47 before fine second over same track/trip from 9lb higher; tailed off on easy ground next time; fair fourth on AW debut in October; wants drying ground.
runners.comment: Shock 80-1 C&D handicap winner on good last July; looks in need of drying ground

runners.name: Patrick Street
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner is 1-23 on turf but hasn't won since 2023; modest form on Flat/hurdles since joining this yard; likely to fall short once again.
runners.comment: Modest form on Flat/hurdles since joining this yard; likely to fall short once again

runners.name: Starting Monday
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; two-time winner but last win came at the Curragh (1m, soft to heavy) off 20lb higher in 2021; couple of decent runs on testing ground last year; well-backed into 7-1 for 26-runner Curragh handicap on return last month but never involved; drops in grade.
runners.comment: Dual winner hasn't won since 2021; well-backed at Curragh on return but didn't run well

runners.name: Deuteronomy
runners.spotlight: Course winner; in the form of his life last June, notching three consecutive wins (1m/1m1f) on varying ground following the addition of visor/blinkers; needed the return run at the Curragh 12 days ago; trainer's daughter Nicola is back on; player down in grade.
runners.comment: In the form of his life last June; needed the return run; Nicola is back on; down in grade

runners.name: I Do Believe
runners.spotlight: Showed little in maidens but was runner-up in Ballinrobe claimer (9.5f, good) last June for Willie McCreery; beaten out of sight on only handicap start for Ian Donoghue; returns for new yard in new headgear.
runners.comment: Second in Ballinrobe claimer for Willie McCreery; tailed off for Ian Donoghue; new yard
runners.owner: Ms Sinead O'Scanaill & Ms Alana McGuinness

runners.name: Prom Queen
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced mare ran a career-best when runner-up in Navan (1m2f, yielding) handicap in October, but was 5l behind the winner; contender on her return if she's ready to roll.
runners.comment: Career-best when runner-up in Navan (1m2f, yielding) handicap in October; chance returning

runners.name: Sarangpur
runners.spotlight: Three undistinguished starts for Peter Lawlor; reared and veered right from stalls when midfield in Navan maiden on yard debut 10 days ago; could improve on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Slow start when midfield in Navan maiden on yard debut; could improve on handicap debut

runners.name: Masego
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise over hurdles last year, notably when fourth in a 3yo hurdle at Listowel in September; well beaten in two previous runs at this venue; worth a market check nonetheless.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two previous runs at this venue; worth a market check nonetheless
runners.owner: Barrow Vale Syndicate/A D Mullins/Ms Margaret Mullins

runners.name: Fantasy Fara
runners.spotlight: Never a factor in trio of hot maidens last year but could prove to be a different proposition on her handicap debut; market will reveal expectations.
runners.comment: Never a factor in trio of hot maidens last year but could be a different proposition now

runners.name: Broadchurch
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three tough maidens; handicaps always going to be his forte and he's one to keep a close eye on in the betting returning from close to a year off the track.
runners.comment: Handicaps always going to be his forte and he's one to keep a close eye on in betting
runners.owner: Jacqueline Moloney/J C Fleming/Crampscastle Bloodstock Ltd

races.race_name: Gowranpark.ie Handicap

runners.name: Rock Of Ireland
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on yard debut over this trip at the Curragh last month under positive ride in testing conditions by a diminishing margin; 8lb higher but remains on a competitive mark and should go well.
runners.comment: Yard debut Curragh winner last month; 8lb rise but relishes these conditions

runners.name: Whimsy
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner in Britain for Andrew Balding; just 1-19 hurdling, quite highly tried at times;  place claims when last-flight faller in Leopardstown handicap last month; possible fitness edge over some of these so don't rule out.
runners.comment: Hurdling of late so possible fitness edge over some of these; don't rule out

runners.name: Robbies Rock
runners.spotlight: Decent dual-purpose sort finished second in last year's Fred Winter Hurdle at Cheltenham and two fine Flat runs on good ground in May, second at Killarney and decisive win in first-time cheekpieces over 1m6f at the Curragh off 78; 9lb higher and ground probably softer than ideal but yard in cracking form and can't be ruled out on return.
runners.comment: Absent since last May's Curragh win; ground softer than ideal but yard in cracking form

runners.name: Star Harbour
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner didn't manage to get his head in front last year but ran well in defeat at times; keen and didn't get home over 1m6f in testing conditions on last month's comeback at Navan; back in trip here so more expected of.
runners.comment: Stamina test too much on recent comeback run; more expected of here

runners.name: Denim And Diamonds
runners.spotlight: Limerick maiden winner at this trip on seasonal debut last June (good) but faced some stiff tasks afterwards in search of black-type on softer ground and soundly beaten in Galway handicap (Granite Bay second) final start in October; ground remains a concern on comeback run.
runners.comment: Maiden win on good, yet to convince on soft

runners.name: Catch Fire
runners.spotlight: French Flat winner at this trip on good ground but nothing so far hurdling for current yard; best watched.
runners.comment: French Flat winner at this trip on good ground but nothing so far hurdling

runners.name: Holy See
runners.spotlight: Useful AW winner in France over 1m/1m1f; campaigned solely over hurdles by current yard, winning Limerick novice on heavy over Christmas, following month okay effort in similar Punchestown contest; first try at this trip on return to the Flat on handicap debut.
runners.comment: French AW wins over shorter; handles ground, trip an unknown on handicap debut

runners.name: Granite Bay
runners.spotlight: Showed good form in 2024 with a trio of wins from 1m2f-1m6f and runner-up on three of last four starts last term, final occasion over this trip at Galway in October; respected on return, market could prove informative to his readiness.
runners.comment: In good form last autumn; market could prove informative to his readiness on return

runners.name: Zoffman
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose 7yo whose losing run on the Flat stretches back to October 2022; absent since down the field in Leopardstown premier handicap in October; okay mark but probably wants a bit better ground.
runners.comment: Long losing Flat run; okay mark but probably wants a bit better ground

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Daring Leader
runners.spotlight: Won at Hamilton (8.5f, good) and by 4.75l at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) last year; dropped away inside the final furlong at the latter track three weeks ago but may take a step forward from that first start for 189 days.
runners.comment: Won twice last year, including on the AW at Newcastle; may have needed his reappearance
runners.owner: D J Jones & M T Brougham

runners.name: Dinah Myte
runners.spotlight: Sole win from nine starts came on heavy ground over 1m at Ffos Las in September; never better than midfield at Southwell (1m, AW) on her return 19 days ago and she's yet to prove herself over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Won on heavy ground over 1m at Ffos Las in September; never better than midfield on return

runners.name: Crafter
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner but he's without a win since November 2024; respectable sixth of 12 in a 0-70 at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) 13 days ago after swinging wide into the straight; drops back down in grade.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; swung wide into the straight when sixth at Lingfield 13 days ago

runners.name: Kaaress
runners.spotlight: Won a classified event at Lingfield (1m2f, AW; first-time visor) in January; held in three starts since, most recently in a classified 15 days ago; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Won a classified event at Lingfield in January but she's been held in three starts since

runners.name: Dors Delight
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 16 starts came in a classified event at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW) last August; solid fifth of eight in a C&D classified event 23 days ago but better is needed back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Won a 1m2f classified event at Chelmsford last August; solid fifth over C&D last time

runners.name: Naughty Niall
runners.spotlight: Course winner in November over 1m4f and he was narrowly denied over C&D in January; respectable fourth of nine here under Ryan Kavanagh 51 days ago and he enters calculations again.
runners.comment: Course winner over 1m4f and a respectable fourth over C&D last time; enters calculations

runners.name: Port Noir
runners.spotlight: In good form this year, winning three of her last five starts (all under Rose Dawes), including over C&D in February; left the impression she's still in form when sixth at Bath (1m, good) four days ago and holds solid claims back on the AW.
runners.comment: Has won three of her last five, including over C&D in February; sixth at Bath latest

runners.name: Eager Puccini
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in each of his six starts (7f/1m) and this longer trip needs to bring out significant improvement.
runners.comment: Well beaten in each of his six starts; longer trip needs to bring out big improvement

runners.name: Ciotog
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner over 8.5f, most recently last January off a 12lb higher mark than today's; hung left when sixth at Bath (1m, good) four days ago and now switches back to the AW with blinkers back on (off since August 2024).
runners.comment: Dual course winner; hung left when sixth over 1m at Bath last time; blinkers now return

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Astrological
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 1m1f/1m2f winner Chourmo (RPR 72); strong in the betting and showed plenty when second of five on his debut over C&D 17 days ago; strong claims with improvement likely.
runners.comment: Strong in the betting when second of five on his debut over C&D 17 days ago; strong claims

runners.name: Zooter
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise when fourth at York (7f, good) and Ripon (1m, good to firm) last summer; failed to build on those efforts on his handicap debut at the latter track in September and has been off since; this longer trip isn't guaranteed to suit on his AW debut but holds solid form claims.
runners.comment: Promise in novice company but failed to build on that on his h'cap debut; off for 194 days

runners.name: Close To Me
runners.spotlight: Out of a smart 2m3f-3m hurdle/chase winner; beaten 37l and 33l in two bumpers, and she's probably best watched on her Flat debut.
runners.comment: Beaten 37l and 33l in two bumpers; probably best watched on her Flat debut

runners.name: Maryland Star
runners.spotlight: Showed little on first two starts but her fifth at Brighton (1m, good to soft) in October wasn't without encouragement; could be suited by this longer trip but improvement is needed on her first start for 184 days.
runners.comment: Fifth at Brighton over 1m in October wasn't without encouragement; off since; up in trip

runners.name: Oceanides
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in a Ludlow bumper on her debut and it's been the same story in two starts on the Flat at this track; others preferred.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two starts on the Flat at this track; others preferred

runners.name: Helios Path
runners.spotlight: Reported to have lost a shoe when tailed off at Bath (1m, soft; 66-1) on his debut in October; cheekpieces go on having been off since and much better is needed over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Tailed off over 1m on his debut at Bath in October; off since and cheekpieces now go on

runners.name: Favourite Friend
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival home in three starts last summer (7f-1m) and will be of greater interest when switched to handicap company.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in three starts last summer (7f-1m); looks one for handicaps

runners.name: King's Courtier
runners.spotlight: Showed little in three starts last year (6f-1m) and has been handed an official rating of 30; steps up in trip with much better needed on his first start since November.
runners.comment: Showed little in three starts last year (6f-1m); steps up in trip with much better needed

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap

runners.name: Kento
runners.spotlight: Gained his third C&D success when narrowly holding on here 16 days ago (Henery Hawk was third); up 3lb but there's no reason why he can't go well again.
runners.comment: Gained his third C&D success 16 days ago (Henery Hawk was third); solid claims up 3lb

runners.name: Nad Alshiba Snow
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden but she's posted some solid efforts since joining this yard, including over C&D; below her best in a classified event at Lingfield when last seen 85 days ago.
runners.comment: 0-16 but has posted some solid efforts for this yard; below her best when last seen

runners.name: Midnight Call
runners.spotlight: Can front-run; won four times here from November to January (5f/6f) but she's been held in fifth here over 6f on her last two starts; may appreciate the drop back down in trip.
runners.comment: Won four times here from November to January; held in fifth over 6f on his last two starts

runners.name: Isla Bella
runners.spotlight: One win from 18 starts but she was narrowly denied by Kento when wearing first-time cheekpieces over C&D last month; didn't get a clear passage when ninth at Southwell a week ago and she can likely bounce back.
runners.comment: Narrowly denied by Kento here last month and her latest effort is worth forgiving; chance

runners.name: Neptune Legend
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner over 6f (has won up to 1m), most recently in November off a 5lb higher mark than today's; ran respectably here over 7f on his first start since returning to Tony Carroll 17 days ago but isn't guaranteed to be suited by this drop back in trip; tongue-tie and blinkers left off.
runners.comment: Has won up to 1m; ran respectably here over 7f on his return to this yard; back in trip

runners.name: Charging Bull
runners.spotlight: Yet to win in six starts and he's been down the field over 6f in two starts since joining this stable; drops back to 5f with a tongue-tie and cheekpieces on for the first time (hood removed).
runners.comment: 0-6; down the field over 6f in two starts for this yard; tongue-tie and cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Comedian Leader
runners.spotlight: All eight of her wins have been over further; came home well over this trip when fifth of nine at Lingfield 20 days ago but will probably be happier back over 6f+.
runners.comment: Came home nicely when fifth over this trip last time but will be happier back over further

runners.name: Henery Hawk
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who has ran well here behind Kento on his last two starts, most recently a close third 16 days ago; holds good claims but his hold-up style means that things need to fall right.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who has ran well here on his last two starts; needs things to fall right

runners.name: Some Nightmare
runners.spotlight: Only one of his nine wins have been on the AW but he posted some solid efforts this winter; third of nine over this C&D 51 days ago and this is his first start since; has claims.
runners.comment: Posted some solid efforts on the AW this winter; third of nine over C&D 51 days ago
runners.owner: Mrs S M Maine & Roger Gould & Partner

runners.name: Coolagh Magic
runners.spotlight: Nine-time AW winner, with the latest success coming at Lingfield (5f, AW) last February; ran well when a close third of nine there 20 days ago and the return of a visor is a positive (last three wins have came in a visor).
runners.comment: Ran well when a close third at Lingfield last time and the return of a visor is a plus
runners.owner: Lady Emma Oshea And A Carson

races.race_name: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Camino Del Ray
runners.spotlight: Built on the encouragement of his comeback effort when winning in first-time cheekpieces at Southwell (1m, AW) 30 days ago, taking his record on the AW to 2-4; claims following a 3lb rise.
runners.comment: Took his record on the AW to 2-4 when winning at Southwell in first-time cheekpieces

runners.name: Pearly Squirrel
runners.spotlight: Doubled her tally when winning here over 7f last month and she ran another good race when a close third of 12 here nine day ago; return to 1m should be okay and she's not discounted.
runners.comment: Won here over 7f last month before another solid effort last time; not discounted

runners.name: Renesmee
runners.spotlight: Got up late to win at Chelmsford (1m, AW) last month and posted a solid effort under a penalty at Newcastle (7f, AW) 29 days ago; stepping back up in trip will suit.
runners.comment: Got up late to win at Chelmsford last month; posted a solid effort under a penalty latest

runners.name: Youarenotforgiven
runners.spotlight: Returned to form when landing a C&D handicap last month and followed up under a 4lb penalty at Lingield (1m) a week later; another 4lb higher here but he can't be dismissed in his bid for the hat-trick.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last month and followed up under a penalty at Lingfield; can't be dismissed

races.race_name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Gold Star Hero
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-3 on the AW when making all at Chelmsford (6f, AW) two weeks ago having been gelded over the winter; 4lb rise for that success looks fair and he has strong claims.
runners.comment: Gelded over the winter and made all at Chelmsford two weeks ago; a 4lb rise looks fair

runners.name: City Cyclone
runners.spotlight: Won here over 7f in February either side of wins over this trip at Kempton; encountered a wide trip when down the field in a higher grade than this last time and he may well be able to bounce back.
runners.comment: Won here over 7f either side of wins over this trip at Kempton; wide trip last time

runners.name: Queue Dos
runners.spotlight: Four-time course winner, most recently over C&D when last seen 104 days ago; respected on her return off this 3lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Gained his fourth course win over C&D 104 days ago; off since and returns off 3lb higher

runners.name: Dyrholaey
runners.spotlight: Gained his fifth win on the AW when landing a class 4 handicap at Chelmsford (6f, AW) in February; below his best in two starts since and now has blinkers on for the first time (replacing cheekpieces).
runners.comment: Below his best in two starts since winning at Chelmsford in February; switch of headgear

runners.name: Artagnan
runners.spotlight: Had a tongue-tie on for the first time when dead-heating at Chelmsford (6f) in October and he ran another solid race off this mark at Southwell (6f) next time; returns from a 160-day break and he could have more to offer on the AW.
runners.comment: Dead-heated at Chelmsford in October and ran well at Southwell next time; off for 160 days

runners.name: Tiger Crusade
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who gained his sixth success on the AW at Lingfield (7f, AW) in January; didn't quite see 1m out when sixth there in February and he never featured in a 0-105 at Kempton (7f) 22 days ago; will appreciate the drop in grade but he'll want a good gallop to aim at over this shorter trip.
runners.comment: C&D winner; will appreciate this drop back down in grade but will need a good gallop

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: On The Inlet
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to posting an improved effort when winning easy on his handicap debut at Southwell (1m, AW) a week ago, coming well clear despite taking a grip; 6lb well in under a penalty and the drop back to 7f shouldn't be an issue; leading chance.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to winning easily on his handicap debut; 6lb well in under a penalty

runners.name: Sup Of Red
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but she caught the eye when fourth of 11 over C&D on her stable debut in February and she was beaten only a head over 8.5f next time; got going too late from off the pace when fifth here 24 days ago and remains of interest.
runners.comment: Shaped well since joining this stable; got going too late from off the pace last time

runners.name: Hello Humphrey
runners.spotlight: Has posted three solid efforts since entering handicaps, most recently when third of eight in a higher grade than this at Kempton (7f, AW) in December; absent since but holds good place claims.
runners.comment: Has posted three solid efforts since handicapping; drops back in grade following a break

runners.name: Lifeguard
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in three starts over 6f/7f for Andrew Balding last year and he was last of six at here over 8.5f on his handicap/stable debut 37 days ago; drops back in trip with a tongue-tie applied; better needed.
runners.comment: Last of six here over 8.5f on his stable/handicap debut last month; tongue-tie added

runners.name: Clear Above
runners.spotlight: Built on his debut effort when fourth at Newcastle (7f, AW) last June and was subsequently gelded; ran to a similar level when third of six at Southwell (7f, AW) on her first start since 12 days ago and now makes her handicap debut; should have more to offer.
runners.comment: Third of six in a maiden at Southwell 12 days ago; now makes his handicap debut

runners.name: Apex Star
runners.spotlight: Down the field on his first two starts and he was beaten 8l when fifth of nine at Chelmsford (6f, AW; hung right) 168 days ago; gelded since and now steps back up to 7f on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Off since finishing fifth of nine at Chelmsford in October; gelded since; handicap debut

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On X Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Siam Ruby
runners.spotlight: Her Lingfield win (1m2f, AW) in January was in a three-runner race but this unexposed filly kicked off her handicap career with a near-miss at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) 11 days ago; went down fighting behind the favourite in that race and she's 2lb well in off the same mark here; big player.
runners.comment: Had a near-miss on handicap debut at Doncaster and she's 2lb well in here; big player

runners.name: Fille Imbassee
runners.spotlight: Won a Lingfield nursery (1m, AW) for Marco Botti in January but she was well held in third of six on her stable debut here (8.6f) last month; that took her record to to 1-6 and she needs more progress to defy this mark.
runners.comment: Record of 1-6 and she needs more progress to defy this mark

runners.name: Naana's Crystal
runners.spotlight: Kingman filly who never landed a blow at Leicester (7f) in sole outing for Alice Haynes; reappeared for new yard with good second at Kempton in February and she went one better when landing the odds at Southwell (1m, Tapeta) seven days later; looks on fair mark now switched to a handicap and this well-bred filly still has potential; interesting contender upped in trip.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell in February and she's open to more progress on handicap debut

runners.name: Della Pace
runners.spotlight: Made it third time lucky when landing a Kempton maiden (1m, Polytrack) last August and she found more progress when fifth of 14 in a valuable series final at Goodwood (7f, good to soft) the following month; returns after 197 days off but she should have more to offer in handicaps this season and she's respected at this new trip.
runners.comment: Good fifth in valuable race in her final 2yo run; respected on handicap/seasonal debut

races.race_name: Boodles Anniversary 4-Y-O Juvenile Hurdle (Grade 1) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Maestro Conti
runners.spotlight: Untroubled to win sole start in France and scored in good style at Kempton (2m, good) and Cheltenham (Grade 2) in first two runs for Dan Skelton; well backed into 5-1 when runner-up in the Triumph Hurdle at Cheltenham (2m1f, soft) last month, when he travelled smoothly for a long way before keeping on well to edge out Minella Study in the battle for second; has leading claims on that form and he should be unfazed by this return to a sharper track.
runners.comment: Travelled smoothly for a long way when runner-up in the Triumph last month; key player

runners.name: Selma De Vary
runners.spotlight: Ran twice on Flat in France and three times in hurdle races at Auteuil, culminating in a win in November (2m1f, heavy); made a promising start for new yard when runner-up behind Narciso Has in a Grade 1 at Leopardstown in February; didn't come from so far back when fourth in the Triumph at Cheltenham last month but she made smooth progress and kept on well after being hampered in the closing stages; that makes her one of the form picks in this field and Willie Mullins has won this twice in the last three years.
runners.comment: Got hampered in closing stages when fourth in the Triumph; big player for Willie Mullins

runners.name: Mange Tout
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to multiple Grade 1 winner Impaire Et Passe; won her first three starts over hurdles before a third in Grade 1 at Leopardstown (2m, heavy) in February; may have more to offer in this sphere but she finished behind Selma De Vary last time and needs to resume progress.
runners.comment: Third in Grade 1 at Leopardstown but was behind Selma De Vary and needs to resume progress

runners.name: Minella Study
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden on the Flat but he's 3-4 over hurdles and travelled strongly for a long way when close third in the Triumph at Cheltenham last month; split Maestro Conti and Selma De Vary in that race and should make another bold bid here; that last run was on soft but his second win was on good.
runners.comment: Close third in the Triumph when he split Maestro Conti and Selma De Vary; respected

runners.name: Winston Junior
runners.spotlight: Useful maiden on the Flat at up 1m5f and he has a record of 2212 since switched to hurdling for his current yard; seems to be learning to settle better and he didn't get a smooth run when an eye-catching second in the juvenile handicap at Cheltenham (2m, good to soft) last month; that was another good step forward and he could continue to progress in this sphere; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Second in the juvenile handicap at Cheltenham and he's open to more progress; interesting
runners.owner: R Bartlett, J Carthy & Mrs P Shanahan

runners.name: Ammes
runners.spotlight: Good Flat horse who won his first two starts over hurdles before a close second behind Minella Study in a Listed race at Wetherby (2m, good) in October; still has potential in this sphere but he was only 12th in the juvenile handicap at Cheltenham last month and needs to resume his progress in this tough race; visor added.
runners.comment: Listed runner-up but he was well held in the juvenile handicap at Cheltenham last month

runners.name: Aqua Bleu
runners.spotlight: Promising second on debut at Hereford (2m, good) in November and he's unbeaten in three runs since; has looked a very willing front-runner but all his wins have been in small-field events and he has a lot to find in this Grade 1 contest.
runners.comment: Front-runner who is 3-4 over hurdles but he has a lot to find in this Grade 1 contest

runners.name: Indian River
runners.spotlight: Promise in his two Irish Flat maidens; justified favouritism in first three starts over hurdles but his limitations were exposed when a never-dangerous tenth in the Triumph at Cheltenham (50-1) last month; passed over.
runners.comment: Triple hurdle winner but his limitations were exposed in the Triumph (tenth) last month

runners.name: Lord
runners.spotlight: Made it 4-6 over hurdles when returning to form with an all-the-way win in a Listed event over C&D (soft) in December; ran to a similar level when runner-up in the Scottish Triumph at Musselburgh in January but this is a much tougher test and he has plenty to find on the figures; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: 4-7 over hurdles but this is a tough test and he has plenty to find on the figures

runners.name: Wolf Rayet
runners.spotlight: Three Flat runs in Ireland and four over hurdles in Britain; ran well at a massive price in the Triumph at Cheltenham (eighth of 20) last month but he was beaten 10l and finished behind three of today's rivals; others preferred.
runners.comment: Ran well at a massive price in the Triumph but beaten 10l and he was behind three of these

races.race_name: William Hill Manifesto Novices' Chase (Grade 1) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lulamba
runners.spotlight: Acts on any ground; ran creditably on the figures but was only third when favourite for the Arkle, in which a mistake two out cost him some ground when challenging; has otherwise been a rising force at about 2m and his record away from the Cheltenham Festival is 6-6, comfortably beating his elders in the Game Spirit for the most recent success; gives the firm impression he's ready for a crack at this new trip; sets the standard and retains the potential for rating higher still.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite in the Arkle; has otherwise been a rising star; this new trip should suit

runners.name: Blueking d'Oroux
runners.spotlight: Useful 2m4f novice chaser but he won a substandard edition of the Rising Stars at Wincanton and his close second at Kempton (with cheekpieces fitted) last time was achieved in a handicap; faces a stiffer task at this level in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Won a substandard Rising Stars; close second in handicap last time; this is a stiffer task

runners.name: Koktail Divin
runners.spotlight: Non-stayer (shaped better than the bare result) in the 3m1f Brown Advisory at Cheltenham having surprisingly swerved the 2m4f Jack Richards option; broadly progressive otherwise and scored by a wide margin at Leopardstown (2m5f, yielding) the time before, recording a very useful RPR; ran well in the Mersey over hurdles at this meeting 12 months ago; remains of major interest, back down in distance.
runners.comment: Non-stayer in the 3m1f Brown Advisory; broadly progressive otherwise; remains of interest

runners.name: Jax Junior
runners.spotlight: Finished 18l behind Lulamba in the Arkle but may fare better in this rematch, with the return to further a likely plus; readily bagged Grade 2 honours at Kempton (2m4f, good to soft) the time before, enhancing his good strike-rate when racing beyond 2m.
runners.comment: Has a good strike-rate when racing beyond 2m, the most recent success in Kempton Grade 2

runners.name: Mambonumberfive
runners.spotlight: Comes here fresh having bypassed the Cheltenham Festival and been absent since a Kingmaker defeat that is perhaps best forgiven due to the heavy ground; 3-3 over fences otherwise, including 2m wins at Aintree (handicap) and Kempton (Grade 2, readily); may resume improvement, provided he stays the new trip.
runners.comment: Best to forgive latest effort; 3-3 over fences otherwise; may resume improvement; new trip

races.race_name: Racing Welfare Bowl Chase (Grade 1) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jango Baie
runners.spotlight: Kept on for close third to Impaire Et Passe in the 2m4f Manifesto here 12 months ago and has improved for longer trips this season; very close fourth in thrilling finish to the King George at Kempton (3m, good) in December and 8l second in the Gold Cup at Cheltenham (3m2f, good to soft) last month; leading claims if that hard race 27 days ago hasn't left a mark.
runners.comment: Runner-up in the Gold Cup; leading claims if that hard race 27 days ago hasn't left a mark

runners.name: Protektorat
runners.spotlight: 11yo whose last Grade 1 win came in March 2024 but he showed strong form when keeping on for second of four to Jonbon in the Melling Chase here (2m4f, good) 12 months ago and has won at Windsor (2m6f, soft; warm conditions race) and Kelso (extended 2m7f, good to soft; Listed) on his two runs this year; he should still be fine at this sort of trip and could be a contender.
runners.comment: 11yo; no Grade 1 win since March 2024 but still capable of strong form; possible contender
runners.owner: Sir A Ferguson, G Mason, L Hales, The Late J Hales

runners.name: Impaire Et Passe
runners.spotlight: Unproven at this trip and reportedly never travelling when pulled up in the Ryanair at Cheltenham last month; however, he's won at this meeting in each of the last two seasons, landing the Grade 1 Manifesto Novices' Chase last April (2m4f, good to soft; Jango Baie third), and he produced a top performance in the Grade 2 Red Mills Chase at Gowran Park this February; could bounce back with big run for last year's winning trainer Willie Mullins.
runners.comment: Pulled up in the Ryanair but 2-2 at this meeting and could bounce back with a big run

runners.name: Pic d'Orhy
runners.spotlight: 11yo who looked as good as ever when runner-up in a captivating battle with Jonbon in the Ascot Chase (2m5f, good to soft) in February, when bidding to win that Grade 1 for the third consecutive year; won the Grade 1 Melling Chase here (2m4f, soft) in 2023; seemed fine at 3m when 1l second in this season's Charlie Hall (good) and his trainer has won this race six times; no surprise to see a bold bid from this front-runner, who is not ruled out.
runners.comment: 11yo; three Grade 1 wins (including here); looked as good as ever last time; not ruled out

runners.name: Spillane's Tower
runners.spotlight: Won two Grade 1s in Ireland in spring 2024 during novice chase campaign (2m4f/3m1f, heavy/yielding) and posted first win since in the Grade 2 Cotswold Chase at Cheltenham (3m1f, soft) this January; he was receiving 6lb from close runner-up L'Homme Presse last time and others in this have achieved more, but he's only eight and could still have improvement to come.
runners.comment: Won Grade 2 Cotswold Chase; others have achieved more but he's open to further improvement

races.race_name: Randox Foxhunters' Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Barton Snow
runners.spotlight: Proved his stamina and made it 5-5 under rules (all in hunter chases) when beating Its On The Line by a neck to win the big one at Cheltenham (3m2f, good to soft) last month, despite a mistake at the final fence; has also won on good going; he doesn't appear to be straightforward but clearly knows how to win and should be fine back down in trip; major player.
runners.comment: Won the big one at Cheltenham last month; should be fine back down in trip; major player

runners.name: Its On The Line
runners.spotlight: Won this in 2024 (heavy); beaten a neck by Barton Snow at last month's Cheltenham Festival (3m2f, good to soft), finishing a close second in that hunter chase for the fourth season in a row; at this trip he may prefer more testing ground than is likely today, but he's still entitled to respect.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024; went close again at Cheltenham last month and he's entitled to respect

runners.name: Unexpected Party
runners.spotlight: Won the Grand Annual at Cheltenham Festival in March 2024 (2m, soft) and runner-up in it last year (good to soft); comfortable wins in hunter chases on last two starts (2m5f/2m6f, good/good to soft); his stamina will be more severely tested in today's hotter company but leading claims if seeing it out.
runners.comment: Comfortable wins over 2m5f/2m6f on last two starts; holds leading form claims

runners.name: Golden Son
runners.spotlight: Useful handicapper who posted easy wins this year in his first two hunter chases (3m/2m7f, good to soft/heavy); 14l fourth at the Cheltenham Festival last month (3m2f, good to soft) so has work to do with some of these, but he was left behind up the hill and is not ruled out back down in trip.
runners.comment: 14l fourth at Cheltenham last month; work to do but the drop back in trip may help
runners.owner: G Mason, A Ferguson And P Nicholls

runners.name: Gracchus De Balme
runners.spotlight: Pulled up at the Cheltenham Festival (3m2f, good to soft) last month; however, he was also pulled up at Leicester last February prior to winning this race at 22-1 (good to soft) so he's capable of bouncing back.
runners.comment: Pulled up at Cheltenham Festival but won this last year and he's capable of bouncing back

runners.name: Snipe
runners.spotlight: Won on his first hunter chase start at Hereford (2m5f, soft) in January and bounced back from a heavy defeat behind Golden Son at Taunton (2m7f, heavy) to win at Leicester (2m, good to soft) last month; however, he needs a clear career best if he's to win this.
runners.comment: 2-3 in hunter chases but he needs a clear career best if he's to win this

runners.name: Thunder Rock
runners.spotlight: Very talented in his prime; this 10yo isn't the force of old and today's race is much tougher than the hunter chases he's won easily on his last two starts (2m4f, good to soft/good) but if he can tap further into his back-class he may well be in the mix; James King has won this twice.
runners.comment: Very talented in his prime; easy hunter chase wins on last two starts; could be in the mix

runners.name: Music Drive
runners.spotlight: He's made the frame at 33-1 in the last two runnings of the Cheltenham Festival hunter chase, finishing about 8l behind Barton Snow and Its On The Line last month when third; tackles a new track today and the drop back in trip isn't an obvious positive, but he could still be an each-way contender.
runners.comment: Again made the frame at 33-1 in the Cheltenham Festival hunter chase; each-way contender

runners.name: Hollywood Harmon
runners.spotlight: Easily won a maiden hunter chase at Cartmel (2m5f, good to soft) last May; didn't run badly at Fakenham (3m, soft) in February but well beaten at Carlisle (2m5f, good to soft) since and she would be a shock winner.
runners.comment: Won maiden hunter chase at Cartmel last May but she would be a shock winner today

runners.name: Willewonga
runners.spotlight: Pulled up at 50-1 in this last year and same outcome at the Cheltenham Festival last month, when 100-1; however, that latest outing was his first run for 110 days and he may have needed it; in front when making a bad blunder at the final fence at Bangor (3m, soft) last February and that form suggests he might not be a forlorn hope each-way, at what should be a big price.
runners.comment: Pulled up at Cheltenham last month but after a break; might not be a forlorn hope each-way

runners.name: Lets Go Champ
runners.spotlight: Won a competitive handicap chase at the Punchestown festival in May 2024 for Henry de Bromhead (2m4f, good to yielding) and winning a hunter chase at Gowran Park (3m1f, heavy) last month showed that he retains considerable ability at the age of 11; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Beat a very solid yardstick for last month's hunter chase win and firmly in calculations

runners.name: Java Point
runners.spotlight: Useful, four-time handicap chase winner who scored at Ludlow (3m, good to soft) in January on his first hunter chase start; runner-up on next two outings (3m/2m7f, soft/good to soft); this 11yo needs a bigger run today.
runners.comment: 11yo who has been in good form since tackling hunter chases but needs a bigger run today

runners.name: A Moments Madness
runners.spotlight: Progressive in handicap chases last spring for Warren Greatrex (2m2f/2m3f, good/soft) and last month's third on stable debut in a hunter chase at Carlisle (2m5f, good to soft), after an absence, can be upgraded as he hung left at that right-handed track; has plenty to find but this 7yo is very lightly raced and, now going left-handed, could go well at sizeable odds.
runners.comment: His comeback third at Carlisle can be upgraded; could go well at sizeable odds

runners.name: Great Notions
runners.spotlight: Won maiden hunter chase at Hexham (3m, good) last May; some good runs in defeat in handicaps since November, most recently 5l second at Sedgefield (3m2f, good), but he has plenty to find today.
runners.comment: Some good runs in defeat in handicaps since November but plenty to find today

runners.name: Densworth
runners.spotlight: Showed the odd snippet of very useful form in 2024, winning easily at Wetherby (2m3f, good) that November on his second chase start; however, his subsequent exploits put him well down the pecking order in this line-up.
runners.comment: The odd snippet of useful form in 2024 but down the pecking order on subsequent evidence

runners.name: Take All
runners.spotlight: Good efforts in defeat behind Java Point (3m, good to soft) and Unexposed Party (2m6f, good to soft) this year and ran well for a long way before fading at last month's Cheltenham Festival (3m2f); could have an each-way shout at big odds now back down in trip; reapplied cheekpieces replace hood.
runners.comment: Ran well for a long way at Cheltenham; could have an each-way shout now back down in trip

runners.name: Fairly Famous
runners.spotlight: Strong form when winning hunter chases at Cheltenham in May 2023 and May 2024; in good heart in point-to-points this season but last May's hunter chase form suggests he may be found wanting.
runners.comment: In good heart in points but last May's hunter chase form suggests he's up against it

runners.name: A Jet Of Our Own
runners.spotlight: Very hard to fancy judged on this year's hunter chase form but he took a big step forward when fifth of 29 at 125-1 in this race last April (good to soft) and is not ruled out each-way back over these obstacles.
runners.comment: Hard to fancy on this year's form but ran well when fifth of 29 at 125-1 in this last term

runners.name: Fan De Blues
runners.spotlight: Very useful for Willie Mullins and ran pretty well over these fences in the 2023 Topham; following a long absence this 11yo hasn't been devoid of promise for his new yard, but needs to take a significant step forward.
runners.comment: Very useful in his prime; not devoid of promise for this yard but needs a step forward

runners.name: Famous Clermont
runners.spotlight: Won this in 2023 (good to soft) and bagged further hunter chase wins in the same Stratford race in May 2024 and 2025 (2m6f, good); won a point last month and he's better than he showed when tailed off in this last April, but this 11yo probably isn't as good as he once was and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Won this in 2023 but this 11yo probably isn't as good as he once was; others are preferred

runners.name: Joker De Mai
runners.spotlight: 7-1 when early faller at the Chair in this last year; this 7yo was an eye-catching third at Ludlow in February on penultimate start, having been hampered, but needs to step up on this season's handicap form if he's to threaten.
runners.comment: Caught the eye two starts ago but he needs to step up on this season's handicap form

runners.name: Gaboriot
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in the Becher here in December and his two subsequent hunter chase runs have yielded an early fall and disappointing defeat; twice placed over these fences, however, most recently in the Grand Sefton over C&D in November, and could have an each-way shout if back on song in change of headgear.
runners.comment: Return to form needed but twice placed over these fences and gets a change of headgear

runners.name: Empire De Maulde
runners.spotlight: Four point wins last year and runner-up in two hunter chases last spring (2m7f/2m6f, good to soft/good); however, he'll need to improve upon that form and this 12yo hasn't shone on either outing this year.
runners.comment: Four point wins last year but this 12yo hasn't shone in 2026

runners.name: Whats The Solution
runners.spotlight: 6-21 in points, his latest win in that sphere coming last month; 12l third of six to Barton Snow at Stratford (2m6f, good) last May in his sole hunter chase and he looks up against it.
runners.comment: Ran okay last May in his sole hunter chase but looks up against it today

runners.name: Enqarde
runners.spotlight: Career highlight was the Tommy Whittle at Haydock in 2021; 50-1 winner of four-runner hunter chase at Leicester (2m6f, soft) this February but pulled up at Exeter (3m, good to soft) since; this 12yo is probably vulnerable.
runners.comment: 50-1 winner at Leicester in February but pulled up at Exeter since; probably vulnerable

races.race_name: William Hill Aintree Hurdle (Grade 1) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Brighterdaysahead
runners.spotlight: Has form on most types of ground; took her Cheltenham Festival record to 0-3 with a defeat in the latest Champion Hurdle but that was a respectable effort without managing to confirm placings with Lossiemouth, who had played second fiddle in the Irish equivalent; record of 2-3 at about 2m4f includes an easy success in the Mersey at this meeting two years ago; very smart mare who holds leading claims at the weights.
runners.comment: Very smart mare; easy winner at this meeting in 2024; has leading claims at the weights

runners.name: The New Lion
runners.spotlight: Broadly progressive; has gone down the Champion Hurdle route this season and ran well in that race most recently, while leaving the impression he's ready for a return to further than 2m; leading novice hurdler at about 2m4f last term, winning the Challow and the Turners (both on good to soft ground); still unbeaten over this sort of distance and commands respect.
runners.comment: Ran well in the Champion Hurdle; leading novice at about 2m4f last term; commands respect

runners.name: Alexei
runners.spotlight: Acts on any ground; good strike-rate includes wins in the Greatwood and Kingwell this season; ties in with Brighterdaysahead and The New Lion on Champion Hurdle running where a late blunder cost him ground when challenging for the places; has form at up to 2m3f; remains a progressive sort.
runners.comment: Good strike-rate; ran well in the Champion Hurdle and remains a progressive sort

runners.name: Lucky Place
runners.spotlight: Went for a handicap option at the latest Cheltenham Festival and was well beaten, albeit with the race not panning out totally smoothly; dual Grade 2 winner last term but still needs to prove he's a Grade 1 hurdler and has the worst chance at the weights in this field.
runners.comment: Still needs to prove he's a Grade 1 hurdler and has the worst chance at the weights
runners.owner: Mrs G Van Geest & M George

runners.name: Golden Ace
runners.spotlight: Fortuitous winner of the 2025 Champion Hurdle (same story in the Fighting Fifth) and finished only fifth, behind some of today's rivals, when attempting to retain her crown; largely consistent and has won over 2m4f but increasing her Grade 1 tally may require further luck.
runners.comment: Registered her Grade 1 wins in fortuitous fashion and is going to need further luck

runners.name: El Fabiolo
runners.spotlight: Probably acts on any going; high class over fences until jumping issues developed; has proved suited by the return to hurdles and an easy win at Leopardstown (2m2f) last time took his record in this sphere to 4-7; the best horse in this field if viewed on his peak chase form.
runners.comment: Latest win took hurdles record to 4-7; the best horse in this if viewed on peak chase form

runners.name: Potters Charm
runners.spotlight: Acts on any ground; the freshest contender in this race, having bypassed the Cheltenham Festival and been absent since landing the National Spirit at Fontwell (2m3f) over six weeks ago when taking hurdles record to 7-11; sole attempt at Aintree resulted in a Grade 1 novice success on Boxing Day 2024; likely to go well.
runners.comment: Record of 7-11 over hurdles; 1-1 at Aintree (Gr 1 novice) and he's the freshest contender

races.race_name: Close Brothers Red Rum Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Sans Bruit
runners.spotlight: His exploits earlier in the season aren't of much concern given his last five wins have come in the month of April, including the last two runnings of this race (soft/good to soft; has also won on good); this prominent racer did it comfortably 12 months ago and is just 3lb higher today; could once more take all the beating.
runners.comment: Has won last two runnings of this race; did it comfortably last year when just 3lb lower

runners.name: Javert Allen
runners.spotlight: Below par at Kempton (2m4f) in February last time but he's run well in defeat otherwise this season, including when fourth over C&D (good to soft) on his reappearance in a race that worked out extremely well; this front-runner could make a bold bid in first-time cheekpieces back at 2m.
runners.comment: Below par last time but in good form otherwise this season and could make a bold bid

runners.name: Highlands Legacy
runners.spotlight: Won on chase debut at Worcester (2m, good) in October and runner-up on next two starts (including C&D) before narrow win at Windsor (2m, soft) in January; he's 6lb higher than last time and in a hotter race today, but this progressive 7yo may be able to find the necessary further improvement.
runners.comment: Enjoying a progressive first campaign over fences; every chance he'll continue to improve

runners.name: Petit Tonnerre
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten ninth in this 12 months ago (good to soft) and tailed off on his last three starts; however, the return to better ground can help and he won a big-field handicap at Punchestown (2m, yielding) last May; on a handy mark judged on that Irish form and he's not discounted.
runners.comment: Tailed off on his last three starts but not discounted in view of last May's Irish win

runners.name: Hercule Du Seuil
runners.spotlight: Won Grade 3 at Killarney (2m4f, yielding) last May for Willie Mullins and he's on a handy mark on that form; however, below par in the winter on final two starts for that yard and soundly beaten in handicaps on both outings for this stable; usual hood is left off; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Ex-Willie Mullins; won Grade 3 chase last May but hard to fancy on more recent evidence

runners.name: Jasko Des Dames
runners.spotlight: Returned to form back on better ground when fifth, beaten just over 10l, in the Grand Annual at Cheltenham (2m, good); he led jumping two out at the Festival and this less demanding track could be right up his street.
runners.comment: Led jumping 2 out in the Grand Annual (when 5th); this less demanding track could be ideal

runners.name: Grain d'Oudairies
runners.spotlight: 10yo who hasn't had much racing for his age and has been progressive this term, with wins at Kelso (2m1f, good to soft) and Haydock (2m4f, good to soft); however, below par at Carlisle in February last time and, even if back on song, there's a firm suspicion this will prove too competitive.
runners.comment: 10yo who has enjoyed a progressive season but this may prove too competitive

runners.name: Inthepocket
runners.spotlight: His fortunes over fences have been mixed but he made good headway in the Grand Annual at Cheltenham (2m, good) last month prior to falling two out; finest hour came when winning Grade 1 Top Novices' Hurdle here (2m, good to soft) in April 2023; could have a big say if avoiding mishap.
runners.comment: Made headway in the Grand Annual prior to falling two out; his finest hour came here

runners.name: Dr T J Eckleburg
runners.spotlight: Runs off a mark 10lb lower than when fifth of 14 in this last year, beaten just over 13l; he's run a couple of reasonable races this season but hasn't shown enough to suggest he's the one to be on today.
runners.comment: 10lb lower than when fifth in this last year but others arrive with more pressing claims

runners.name: Wonleg
runners.spotlight: Won Wexford handicap (2m, good) last July on third chase start and returned to form when third of 20 at Leopardstown (2m1f, yielding) in December; needs a career best today but this unexposed 6yo represents a top Irish trainer (who saddles three) and could yet have more to offer.
runners.comment: Unexposed 6yo who represents a top Irish trainer and may yet have more to offer

runners.name: Palamon
runners.spotlight: Well-backed, even-money favourite at Bangor (2m4f) last Tuesday, when reverting to good ground in a reapplied tongue-tie and returning to form with a convincing win; 9lb higher in a much hotter race today but not ruled out for his top yard on the back of that stylish success.
runners.comment: Well backed when returning to form with ready win at Bangor; not ruled out off 9lb higher

runners.name: Brookie
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in Ascot handicap in November and tailed off on sole outing since in the Champion Chase last month; it's hard to say what sort of form he's in but this C&D brought out the very best in him when runner-up in the Grade 1 Maghull Novices' Chase (good) 12 months ago; could go well back here.
runners.comment: Hard to say what sort of form he's in but this C&D brought out very best in him last April

runners.name: Ryan's Rocket
runners.spotlight: Firmly in the picture judged on his handicap win at Newbury (2m, good to soft) in November and he drops back in trip today having weakened over 2m4f at Kempton last month; not ruled out but he made mistakes last time and has unseated on two of his last four starts; needs to cut out the blunders.
runners.comment: Firmly in the picture on his November win but his jumping might be put under pressure here

runners.name: Stencil
runners.spotlight: 5yo; trained in France but campaigned in Britain since start of 2025; impressed in a warm novice chase at Chepstow (2m, soft) in January and 25l tenth in the Jack Richards at Cheltenham (2m4f, good) was better than the bare facts, as he pulled hard and was held up in a race that favoured those who were up with the pace; could settle better now back down in trip; interesting.
runners.comment: 5yo who ran better than the bare facts at Cheltenham; interesting now back down in trip

runners.name: Boothill
runners.spotlight: Ascot specialist who runs at Aintree for the first time; following wind surgery he ran okay when 13l eighth of 20 in the Grand Annual at Cheltenham (2m, good) last month, and he's another 4lb lower today; however, this 11yo is without a win since 2023 and needs to find something extra now in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 8th in the Grand Annual and 4lb lower today, but this 11yo needs to find something extra
